verses.sanskrit: वैशम्पायन उवाच द्वा:स्थं प्राह महाप्राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः। विदुरं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि तमिहानय मा चिरम्॥
verses.translation: Vaishampayana said The Lord of the Earth Dhritarashtra of great wisdom, said to his attendant-I desire to see Vidura. Bring him here without delay.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहितो धृतराष्ट्रेण दूतः क्षत्तारमब्रवीत्। ईश्वरस्त्वां महाराजो महाप्राज्ञ दिदृक्षति॥
verses.translation: Being sent by Dhritarashtra the messenger said to the Kshatriya-"O you of great wisdom, my lord, the king wants to see you."

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु विदुरः प्राप्य राजनिवेशनम्। अब्रवीद् धृतराष्ट्राय द्वा:स्थ मां प्रतिवेदय॥
verses.translation: Being thus addressed, Vidura, having come to the royal palace, said to the gate keeper-'Announce me to Dhritarashtra.'

verses.sanskrit: द्वाःस्थ उवाच विदुरोऽयमनुप्राप्तो राजेन्द्र तव शासनात्। द्रष्टुमिच्छति ते पादौ किं करोतु प्रशाधिमाम्॥
verses.translation: The gate keeper said O chief among kings, Vidura, having come here at your command, desires to behold your feet. Command me what he is to do.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच प्रवेशय महाप्राज्ञं विदुरं दीर्घदर्शिनम्। अहं हि विदुरस्यास्य नाकल्यो जातु दर्शने॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said Let the very wise Vidura endued with great foresight enter. I am always willing and prepared to see Vidura.

verses.sanskrit: द्वा:स्थ उवाच प्रविशान्तःपुरं क्षत्तर्महाराजस्य धीमतः। नहि ते दर्शनेऽकल्पो जातु राजाऽब्रवीद्धि माम्॥
verses.translation: The gate keeper said O Kshattri, enter the inner apartments of the wise king; the king told me that he was never unwilling to see you.

verses.sanskrit: वैशम्पायन उवाच ततः प्रविश्य विदुरो धृतराष्ट्रनिवेशनम्। अब्रवीत् प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं चिन्तयानं नराधिपम्॥
verses.translation: Vaishampayana said Then Vidura having entered the palace of Dhritarashtra said with clasped hands to the lord of men who was absorbed in thought.

verses.sanskrit: विदुरोऽहं महाप्राज्ञ सम्प्राप्तस्तव शासनात्। यदि किंचन कर्तव्यमयमस्मि प्रशाधि माम्॥
verses.translation: O you of great wisdom, I am Vidura, come here by your command, if there is anything to be done, here I am; command me.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच संजयो विदुर प्राज्ञो गर्हयित्वा च मां गतः। अजातशत्रोःश्वो वाक्यं सभामध्ये स वक्ष्यति॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said O Vidura, Sanjaya has arrived here and having found fault with me gone away; the message of Yudhishthira he will deliver in the council.

verses.sanskrit: तस्याद्य कुरुवीरस्य न विज्ञातं वचो मया। तन्मे दहति गात्राणि तदकार्षित् प्रजागरम्॥
verses.translation: This day was I unable to know (the nature of) the message of the hero among the Kurus; therefore is my body burning, which has caused sleeplessness.

verses.sanskrit: जाग्रतो दह्यमानस्य श्रयो यदनुपश्यसि। तद् ब्रूहि त्वं हि नस्तात धर्मार्थकुशलो ह्यसि॥
verses.translation: What you consider good for a man who gets no sleep and whose body is burning, tell (me) you are versed in what is regions and what leads to profit.

verses.sanskrit: यतः प्राप्त: संजयः पाण्डवेभ्यो न मे यथावन्मनसः प्रशान्तिः। सर्वेन्द्रियाण्यप्रकृतिं गतानि किं वक्ष्यतीत्येव मेऽद्य प्रचिन्ता॥
verses.translation: Since Sanjaya has come back from the Pandavas, I have not my usual calmness of mind; all my senses are in disorders I am thinking what he will say.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच अभियुक्तं बलवता दुर्बलं हीनसाधनम्। हृतस्वं कामिनं चोरमाविशन्ति प्रजागराः॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Sleeplessness overpowers one attacked by a stronger man, the weak, those who have failed to attain an end, those whose wealth has been stolen, those fired with desire and thieves.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिदेतैर्महादोषैर्न स्पृष्टोऽसि नराधिप। कच्चिच्च परवित्तेषु गृद्धन् न परितप्यसे।॥
verses.translation: I hope, O Lord of men, you have not been affected by any of these grave evils nor are you possessed by covetousness at other people's wealth.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच श्रोतुमिच्छामि ते धर्म्यं परं नैःश्रेयसं वचः। अस्मिन् राजर्षिवंशे हि त्वमेकः प्राज्ञसम्मतः॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said I desire to hear words from you that are at once moral in spirit and beneficial; in this race i of royal devotees; you are the only one ; hundred by the wise.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच निषेवते प्रशस्तानि निन्दितानि न सेवते। अनास्तिकः श्रद्दधान एतत् पण्डितलक्षणम्॥ राजालक्षण संपन्नस्त्रैलोकस्याधिपो भवेत्।
verses.translation: Vidura said He is said to be a wise man, whom for a consciousness of the power of one's own self, exertion, forbearance and constancy in religion does not draw aside from the acquisition of the high ends in life.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मज्ञानं समारम्भणततिक्षा धर्मनित्यता। यमर्थान्नापकर्षन्ति स वै पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said to be a wise man whom anger, exultation, pride, shame, stupefaction and vanity cannot draw aside from the acquisition of the high ends in life.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य कृत्यं न जानन्ति मन्त्रं वा मन्त्रितं परे। कृतमेवास्य जानन्ति स वै पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said to be a wise man whose proposed acts, line of action agreed on are not known to other and known only after they have been put into execution.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य कृत्यं न विघ्नन्ति शीतमुष्णं भयं रतिः। समृद्धिरसमृद्धिर्वा स वै पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is called a wise man whose acts are not hampered by cold, heat, fear, lust prosperity or adversity.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य संसारिणी प्रज्ञा धर्मार्थावनुवर्तते। कामादर्थं वृणीते यः स वै पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is called a wise man whose wisdom naturally follows both religion and profit and who chooses such course of life as is of use in both the worlds and not acts immediately resulting in pleasure.

verses.sanskrit: यथाशक्ति चिकीर्षन्ति यथाशक्ति च कुर्वते। न किंचिदवमन्यन्ते नराः पण्डितबुद्धयः॥
verses.translation: Wise men exert to the best of their ability and also act to the best of their ability and they disregard nothing.

verses.sanskrit: क्षिप्रं विजानाति चिरं शृणोति विज्ञाय चार्थं भजते न कामात्। नासम्पृष्टो व्युपयुक्ते परार्थे तत् प्रज्ञानं प्रथमं पण्डितस्य॥
verses.translation: It the wisdom of the foremost wise man to understand quickly, listen pertinently and having understood the effects of action not to follow them from desire to get pleasure (but from judgment) and not to connect one's self with other peoples' affairs unsolicited.

verses.sanskrit: नाप्राप्यमभिवाञ्छन्ति नष्टं नेच्छन्ति शोचितुम्। आपत्सु च न मुह्यन्ति नराः पण्डितबुद्धयः॥
verses.translation: Men with the wisdom of a wise man do not strive for the unattainable, do not grieves for what is lost and do not lose their senses in calamities.

verses.sanskrit: निश्चित्य यः प्रक्रमते नान्तर्वसति कर्मणः। अवन्ध्यकालो वश्यात्मा स वै पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said to be a wise man who exerts after having decided on a courts of action and who dose not stop in the middle, who dose not waste his time and who has his self under control.

verses.sanskrit: आर्यकर्मणि रज्यन्ते भूतिकर्माणि कुर्वते। हितं च नाभ्यसूयन्ति पण्डिता भरतर्षभ॥
verses.translation: Wise men rejoice in virtuous deeds and do those that tend to their prosperity and look not with contempt on what is good.

verses.sanskrit: न हृष्यत्यात्मसम्माने नावमानेन तप्यते। गाङ्गो ह्रद इवाक्षोभ्यो यः स पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said be a wise man who dose not exult in honors to himself and grieves not at intuits and who remains unagitated like a lake near the Ganga.

verses.sanskrit: तत्त्वज्ञः सर्वभूतानां योगज्ञः सर्वकर्मणाम्। उपाज्ञो मनुष्याणां नरः पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: That man is said to be wise who is cognizant of the nature of all creatures (their ultimate destructibility), of the connections (causes and effects) of all acts and the means of human beings (employed in the attainment of their ends).

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्तवाक् चित्रकथ ऊहवान् प्रतिभानवान्। आशु ग्रन्थस्य वक्ता च यः स पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said to be a wise man who speaks boldly, can talk on diverse subjects, can argue well, has genius and who can explain the meaning of what is written in books at once.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतं प्रज्ञानुगं यस्य प्रज्ञा चैव श्रुतानुगा। असम्भिन्नार्यमर्यादः पण्डिताख्यां लभेत सः॥
verses.translation: He gains the reputation of wise man who regulates his studies by wisdom and whose wisdom follows the books, who is ever ready to respect those that are good.

verses.sanskrit: अश्रुतश्च समुन्नद्धो दरिद्रश्च महामनाः। अर्थाश्चाकर्मणा प्रेप्सुर्मूढ इत्युच्यते बुधैः॥
verses.translation: Wise men call them fools, who though not learned are haughty, though poor are vain and who are who are are desirous of attaining prosperity by wrong acts.

verses.sanskrit: स्वमर्थं यः परित्यज्य परार्थमनुतिष्ठति। मिथ्या चरति मित्रार्थं यश्च मूढः स उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said be a fool who neglecting his own interests looks after those of others and who resorts to deceit for (serving) his friends.

verses.sanskrit: अकामान् कामयति यः कामयानान् परित्यजेत्। बलवन्तं च यो द्वेष्टि तमाहुर्मूढचेतसम्॥
verses.translation: He is said to be foolish-minded who desires what ought not to be desired and forsakes those that are fit to be desired and who treats stronger parties with contempt.

verses.sanskrit: अमित्रं कुरुते मित्रं मित्रं द्वेष्टि हिनस्ति च। कर्म चारभते दुष्टं तमाहुर्मूढचेतसम्॥
verses.translation: He is said be foolish-minded who makes friends of those that are foes and who hates and slays that are well-wishers and who commits wicked acts.

verses.sanskrit: संसारयति कृत्यानि सर्वत्र विचिकित्सते। चिरं करोति क्षिप्रार्थं स मूढो भरतर्षभ।॥
verses.translation: He is a fool who gives out his intended acts, who has doubts in every case and who delays in doing things that ought to be done quickly.

verses.sanskrit: श्राद्धं पितृभ्यो न ददाति दैवतानि न चार्चति। सुहन्मित्रं न लभते तमाहुर्मूढचेतसम्॥
verses.translation: He is said to be foolish-minded who dose not give offerings to his ancestors, (Pitris), who dose not worship gods and who dose not (manage to) get good-hearted friends.

verses.sanskrit: अनाहूतः प्रविशति अपृष्टो बहु भाषते। अविश्वस्त विश्वसिति मूढचेता नराधमः॥
verses.translation: That fool the worst of men who enters unasked, speaks much unsolicited and has; confidence in those that are untrust-worthy.

verses.sanskrit: परं क्षिपति दोषेण वर्तमानः स्वयं तथा। यश्च क्रुध्यत्यनीशानः स च मूढतमो नरः॥
verses.translation: That man is the worst of fools who throws the blame on others, though himself is to blame and who is though unable to do anything.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मनो बलमज्ञाय धर्मार्थपरिवर्जितम्। अलभ्यमिच्छन् नैष्कर्म्यान्मूढबुद्धिरिहोच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said to be foolish-headed in this world who is desirous of an object hard to gain without knowing his own strength, without employing adequate means for it and the object if gained securing him neither merit in heaven nor profit in this world.

verses.sanskrit: अशिष्यं शास्ति यो राजन् यश्च शून्यमुपासते। कदर्यं भजते यश्च तमाहुर्मूढचेतसम्॥
verses.translation: He is said to be foolish-minded who punishes, Oking him who deserves not punishment, who flatters big people without their knowledge and who attends on misers.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थं महान्तमासाद्य विद्यामैश्वर्यमेव वा। विचरत्यसमुन्नद्धो यः स पण्डित उच्यते॥
verses.translation: He is said to be a wise man who, having acquired immense wealth, learning or power, conducts himself without any haughtiness.

verses.sanskrit: एकः सम्पन्नमश्नाति वस्ते वासश्च शोभनम्। योऽसंविभज्य भृत्येभ्यः को नृशंसतरस्ततः॥
verses.translation: Who is meaner then he who though possessed of riches eats and wears pretty looking dresses alone without sharing them amongst his dependents.

verses.sanskrit: एकः पापानि कुरुते फलं भुङ्क्ते महाजनः। भोक्तारो विप्रमुच्यन्ते कर्ता दोषेण लिप्यते॥
verses.translation: One man dose wicked deeds while the fruit is enjoyed by many; others enjoy while the doer alone is blamed.

verses.sanskrit: एकं हन्यान्न वा हन्यादिषुर्मुक्तो धनुष्मता। बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतोत्सृष्टा हन्याद् राष्ट्र सराजकम्॥
verses.translation: Only one may or may not be killed by a bowman who has shot an arrow but when a wise man employs his wisdom (viciously) a kingdom may be destroyed along with the king.

verses.sanskrit: एकया द्वे विनिश्चित्य त्रीश्चतुर्भिर्वशे कुरु। पञ्च जित्वा विदित्वा षट् सप्त हित्वा सुखी भव।।४४
verses.translation: Discriminating the two (right and wrong or the transient and the permanent effect of actions) by means of the one (intellect), bring under your control the three (friend, foe and one who is indifferent in these respects or desire, anger and greed) by means of four (Conciliation, gift dissension and punishment) and subjugating the five (senses) and knowing the six (treaty, war, hunger, thirst, calamity, imbecility, old age and death or desire anger, greed, &c) and keeping yourself away from the seven (women, dice, hunting, drink, harshness of speech severity of punishment and misuse of wealth) be happy.

verses.sanskrit: एकं विषरसो हन्ति शस्त्रेणैकश्च बध्यते। सराष्ट्र सप्रजं हन्ति राजानं मन्त्रविप्लवः॥
verses.translation: Poison kills but one and also one is slain by a weapon, while disagreement between ministers destroys a king with the kingdom and the subjects.

verses.sanskrit: एकः स्वादु न भुञ्जीत एकश्चार्थान् न चिन्तयेत्। एको न गच्छेदध्वानं नैकः सुप्तेषु जागृयात्॥
verses.translation: Alone one should not taste a delicious dish, alone one should not think of profitable undertakings, alone one should not go on a journey and alone one should not be awake amidst those that are asleep.

verses.sanskrit: एकमेवाद्वितीयं तद् यद् राजन् नावबुध्यसे। सत्यं स्वर्गस्य सोपानं पारावारस्य नौरिव॥
verses.translation: The Being, who has no rival, whom you have not been able to comprehend, is Truth and the Way to Heaven, like a boat in the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: एकः क्षमावतां दोषो द्वितीयो नोपपद्यते। यदेनं क्षमया युक्तमशक्तं मन्यते जनः॥
verses.translation: There is but one defect in persons of a forgiving disposition; since people consider this man of a foregoing nature to be weak.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽस्य दोषो न मन्तव्यः क्षमा हि परमं बलम्। क्षमा गुणो ह्यशक्तानां भूषणं क्षमा॥
verses.translation: This defect in such a man need not be made too much of; forgiveness is a great power. For the weak as well as for the strong forgiveness is an ornament.

verses.sanskrit: क्षमा वशीकृतिलोक क्षमया किं न साध्यते। शान्तिखङ्गः करे यस्य किं करिष्यति दुर्जनः॥
verses.translation: Forgiveness subdues (everything) in the world. What is there that cannot be accomplished by forgiveness? What can a wicked man do to one who has the sword of pacification in his hand.

verses.sanskrit: अतृणे पतितो वह्निः स्वयमेवोपशाम्यति। अक्षमावान् परं दोषेरात्मानं चैव योजयेत्॥
verses.translation: Fire, falling on ground devoid of vegetation, is extinguished of itself. The unforgiving poison defiles himself with grave defects.

verses.sanskrit: एको धर्मः परं श्रेयः क्षमैका शान्तिरुत्तमा विद्यैका परमा तृप्तिरहिंसैका सुखावहा॥
verses.translation: Virtue is the only highest good forgiveness the supreme peace, knowledge the deepest satisfaction and benevolence the one cause of happiness.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ ग्रसते भूमिः सर्पो बिलशयानिवा राजानं चाविरोद्धारं ब्राह्मणं चाप्रवासिनम्।।५३
verses.translation: The earth devours these two, as a serpent destroys living in holes viz., a king who is not a warrior and a Brahmana who has never been away form home (to holy places).

verses.sanskrit: द्वे कर्मणी नरः कुर्वन्नस्मिल्लोके विरोचते। अब्रुवन् परुषं किंचिदसतोऽनर्चयंस्तथा॥
verses.translation: By doing two things a man can attain to renown in this world by not indulging in harshness of speech and by disregarding the slightly his honest.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषव्याघ्र परप्रत्ययकारिणौ। स्त्रियः कामितकामिन्यो लोकः पूजितपूजकः॥
verses.translation: These two, 0 best of men, place confidence in others viz. The women who desire men (only because the latter are) desired by other women and the men who worship another (only because the latter is) worshipped by others.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ कण्टको तीक्ष्णौ शरीरपरिशोषिणौ। यश्चाधनः कामयते यश्च कुप्यत्यनीश्वरः॥
verses.translation: These two are like sharp thorns consuming the body viz. the man, who being poor, has desires and one, who thought powerless, is yet wrathful.

verses.sanskrit: द्वावेव न विराजेते विपरीतेन कर्मणा। गृहस्थश्च निरारम्भः कार्यवांश्चैव भिक्षुकः॥
verses.translation: These two cannot shine owing to the inconsistency of their acts with their station viz., the householder who does not exert and the begger who is busy.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ राजन् स्वर्गस्योपरि तिष्ठतः। प्रभुश्च क्षमया युक्तो दरिद्रश्च प्रदानवान्॥
verses.translation: These two men, O king, are placed above heaven, viz. the powerful man graced with forgiveness and the poor man with charity.

verses.sanskrit: न्यायागतस्य द्रव्यस्य बोद्धव्यौ द्वावतिक्रमौ। अपात्रे प्रतिपत्तिश्च पात्रे चाप्रतिपादनम्॥
verses.translation: Of things earned by just means these two must be looked upon as abuses viz. gifts to the unworthy and refusal to the worthy.

verses.sanskrit: द्वावम्भसि निवेष्टव्यौ गले बद्ध्वा दृढां शिलाम्। धनवन्तमदातारं दरिद्रं चातपस्विनम्॥
verses.translation: These two are fit to be thrown into the water with stones firmly bound to their necks viz. the wealthy man who does not make gifts and the poor man who is a devotee.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषव्याघ्र सूर्यमण्डलभेदिनौ। परिवाड् योगयुक्तश्च रणे चाभिमुखो हतः॥
verses.translation: These two, O best of men, pierce the orb of the sun viz. an itinerant versed in yoga and one slain when engaged in battle.

verses.sanskrit: त्रयो न्याया मनुष्याणां श्रूयन्ते भरतर्षभ। कनीयानं मध्यमः श्रेष्ठ इति वेदविदो विदुः॥
verses.translation: Men are known to have three means (for attaining of their ends) 0 best the race of Bharatas, they are kuown to people versed in the Vedas as the bad, the middling and the good.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिविधाः पुरुषा राजन्नुत्तमाधममध्यमाः। नियोजयेद् यथावत् तांस्त्रिविधेष्वेव कर्मसु॥
verses.translation: Men too, o king, of three denominations viz., the good, bad middling; they should therefore be employed in their respective avocations-in three sorts of work. are

verses.sanskrit: त्रय एवाधना राजन् भार्या दासस्तथा सुतः। यत् ते समधिगच्छन्ति यस्य ते तस्य तद् धनम्॥
verses.translation: The three are without wealth, O king, viz., the wife, the slave and the son; what ever they earn beings to him to whom they belong.

verses.sanskrit: हरणं च परस्वानां परदाराभिमर्शनम्। सुहृदश्च परित्यागस्त्रयो दोषाः क्षयावहाः॥
verses.translation: Robbing other people of their wealth, outraging other people's wives and forsaking a friend-these three sins are consuming.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः। कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत् त्रयं त्यजेत्॥
verses.translation: These three are the way to hell and destruction of self viz., desire, anger and greed; therefore should these three be renounced.

verses.sanskrit: वरप्रदानं राज्यं च पुत्रजन्म च भारत। शत्रोश्च मोक्षणं कृच्छ्रात् त्रीणि चैकं च तत्समम्॥
verses.translation: These three viz., a follower, one who seeks your protection saying I am your and one who has come to your house should never be forsaken, even in times of grave danger.

verses.sanskrit: भक्तं च भजमानं च तवास्मीति च वादिनम्। त्रीनेतांश्छरणं प्राप्तान् विषमेऽपि न संत्यजेत्॥
verses.translation: The grant of a boon, of a kingdom and the begetting of a son, O son of Bharata, these three are equaled by the release of foe from difficulty alone.

verses.sanskrit: चत्वारि राज्ञा तु महाबलेन वान्याहुः पण्डितस्तानि विद्यात्। न दीर्घसूत्रै रभसैश्चारणैश्च॥
verses.translation: These four should be excluded from counsels even by a greatly powerful king-the learned men have said men of little sense should not be consulted-nor men who make unnecessary delays, iazy men and flatterers.

verses.sanskrit: चत्वारि ते तात गृहे वसन्तु श्रियाभिजुष्टस्य गृहस्थधर्मे। वृद्धो ज्ञातिरवसन्नः कुलीनः सखादरिद्रो भगिनी चानपत्या॥
verses.translation: Let these four dwell at your house, O sire, who a householder crowned with are prosperity; viz., aged and worn out cousins, men of noble families, destitute friends and childless sisters.

verses.sanskrit: चत्वार्याह महाराज साधस्कानि बृहस्पतिः। पृच्छते त्रिदशेन्द्राय तानीमानि निबोध मे॥
verses.translation: Four things, O great king, were declared to bear fruit instantaneously by Brihaspati on being asked by the chief of the celestial; they are these, listen to me.

verses.sanskrit: देवतानां च संकल्पमनुभावं च धीमताम्। विनयं कृतविद्यानां विनाशं पापकर्मणाम्॥
verses.translation: The resolve of the gods, the intellect, of the wise, the humility of the accomplished and the destruction of wicked deeds.

verses.sanskrit: चत्वारि कर्माण्यभयंकराणि भयं प्रयच्छन्त्ययथाकृतानि। मानाग्निहोत्रमुत मानमौनं मानेनाधीतमुत मानयज्ञः॥
verses.translation: Four things calculated to remove fear, cause fear when improperly done-Agnihotra, the vow of silence, the vow of study and that of sacrificial ceremonies.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चाग्नयो मनुष्येण परिचर्याः प्रयत्नतः। पिता माताग्निरात्मा च गुस्श्च भरतर्षभ॥
verses.translation: Five fires should be worshipped by a man with care viz., father, mother, Agni, self and spiritual guide, O best the race of Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चैव पूजयँल्लोके यशः प्राप्नोति केवलम्। देवान् पितॄन् मनुष्यांश्च भिक्षूनतिथिपञ्चमान्॥
verses.translation: By worshipping these five viz., the gods, the ancestors, men, beggars and guest a man obtains renown in this world.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्च त्वानुगमिष्यन्ति यत्र यत्र गमिष्यसि। मित्राण्यमित्रा मध्यस्था उपजीव्योपजीविनः॥
verses.translation: These five follow you wherever you go viz., friends, those that are neither, dependents nor those who ought to be supported by you.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चेन्द्रियस्य मर्त्यस्यच्छिद्रं चेदेकमिन्द्रियम्। ततोऽस्य स्रवति प्रज्ञा दृतेः पात्रादिवोदकम्॥
verses.translation: Of the five senses in this world, if one has a hole, them from that hole gets out the wisdom of an intelligent man like water, from a pot.

verses.sanskrit: षड् दोषाः पुरुषेणेह हातव्या भूतिमिच्छता। निद्रा तन्द्रा भयं क्रोध आलस्यं दीर्घसूत्रता॥
verses.translation: These six defects should be shunned by a man in this world who desires for prosperity viz., sleep drowsiness, fear, anger, laziness and procrastination.

verses.sanskrit: घडिमान् पुरुषो जह्याद् भिन्नां नावमिवार्णवे। अप्रवक्तारमाचार्यमनधीयानमृत्विजम्॥ अरक्षितारं राजानं भार्यां चाप्रियवादिनीम्। ग्रामकामं च गोपालं वनकामं च नापितम्॥
verses.translation: These six should be avoided by a man like a broken boat in the sea; a tutor that cannot interpret the meaning of the holy books, a priest that is not well read, a king that cannot protect a wife given to the use of disagreeable words, a cowherd that desires always for the village (and not the pasture) and barber that desires for the wilderness.

verses.sanskrit: घडेव तु गुणाः पुंसा न हातव्याः कदाचन। सत्यं दानमनालस्यमनसूया क्षमा धृतिः॥
verses.translation: The six qualities should never be renounced by a man viz, truth, charity, the reverse of laziness, benevolence, forgiveness and patience.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थागमो नित्यमरोगिता च प्रिया च भार्या प्रियवादिनी च। वश्यश्च पुत्रोऽर्थकरी च विद्या षड् जीवलोकस्य सुखानि राजन्॥
verses.translation: These six are destroyed through a moment's neglect viz., COWS, service, agriculture, wife, book learning and the prosperity of a Shudra.

verses.sanskrit: घण्णामात्मनि नित्यानामैश्वर्यं योऽधिगच्छति। न स पापैः कुतोऽनथैर्युज्यते विजितेन्द्रियः॥ षडिमे षट्सु जीवन्ति सप्तमो नोपलभ्यते। चौराः प्रमत्ते जीवन्ति व्याधितेषु चिकित्सकाः॥
verses.translation: These six always cease to care those who have previously done them good viz., a learned pupil, the tutor, he who has got a wife the mother, one whose desire has been satisfied, the woman, one whose wants have been removed, the giver, one who has crossed a river, boat and the diseased (who has been cured) and the physician.

verses.sanskrit: प्रमदाः कामयानेषु यजमानेषु याजकाः। राजा विवदमानेषु नित्यं मूर्खेषु पण्डिताः॥
verses.translation: Immunity from malady, unindebtedness, the reverse of exile, coming in contact with good men, confidence in one's own livelihood and life without fear-these six, constitute the happiness of men, O king.

verses.sanskrit: पडिमानि विनश्यन्ति मुहूर्तमनवेक्षणात्। गाव: सेवा कृषिर्भार्या विद्या वृषलसंगतिः॥
verses.translation: The curious the malicious, the discontented, the wrathful, the ever suspicious and those living on other people's fortunesthese six are alwu miserable.

verses.sanskrit: षडेते ह्यवमन्यन्ते नित्यं पूर्वोपकारिणम्। आचार्य शिक्षिताः शिष्याः कृतदाराश्च मातरम्॥
verses.translation: The attainment of prosperity being always healthy, a beloved wife of sweet speech, an obedient son and knowledge tending to prosperity these six constitute the happiness of men, Oking.

verses.sanskrit: नारी विगतकामास्तु कृतार्थाश्च प्रयोजकम्। नावं निस्तीर्णकान्तारा आतुराश्च चिकित्सकम्॥
verses.translation: He who attains lordship over the six (desire, anger, grief, stupefaction, pride and vanity) that master of the senses never commits sins-how can he be smitten by grief.

verses.sanskrit: आरोग्यमानृण्यमविप्रवासः सद्भिर्मनुष्यैः सह सम्प्रयोगः। स्वप्रत्यया वृत्तिरभीतवासः षड् जीवलोकस्य सुखानि राजन्॥ ईयो घृणी नसंतुष्टः क्रोधनो नित्यशङ्कितः। परभाग्योपजीवी च षडेते नित्यदुःखिताः॥
verses.translation: These six live on six (others) and not on a seventh viz., thieves live on the absent-minded, physicians on the diseased, women on those affected with desire, priests on them that perform sacrifices, kings on persons that quarrel and wise men on the ignorant.

verses.sanskrit: सप्त दोषाः सदा राज्ञा हतव्या व्यसनोदयाः। प्रायशो यैर्विनश्यन्ति कृतमूला अपीश्वराः॥
verses.translation: Seven defects which are the sources of calainity should also be avoided by a king since they always accomplish the destruction of even firmly established king.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रियोऽक्षा मृगया पानं वाक्पारुष्यं च पञ्चमम्। महच्च दण्डपारुष्यमर्थदूषणमेव च॥
verses.translation: (They are) women, dice, game, drink, harshness of speech, these five and the great severity of punishment and abuse of wealth.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टौ पूर्वनिमित्तानि नरस्य विनशिष्यतः। ब्राह्मणान् प्रथमं द्वेष्टि ब्राह्मणैश्च विरुध्यते॥ ब्राह्मणस्वानि चादत्ते ब्राह्मणांश्च जिघांसति। रमते निन्दया चैषां प्रशंसां नाभिनन्दति॥ नैनान् स्मरति कृत्येषु याचितश्चाभ्यसूयति। एतान् दोषान् नरः प्राज्ञो बुध्येद् बुद्ध्वा विसर्जयेत्।।
verses.translation: Eight preliminary causes ruin a man; despising the Brahmanas, the struggle with the Brahmanas, the acquirement of a Brahmana's wealth taking the life of Brahmana, rejoicing at reviling them, disapproval of praise to them, not remembering them on occasions of festivity and finding fault with them when they ask for anything. These defects should a wise man understanding them, should avoid.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टाविमानि हर्षस्य नवनीतानि भारत। वर्तमानानि दृश्यन्ते तान्येव स्वसुखान्यपि॥ समागमश्च सखिभिर्महांश्चैव धनागमः। पुत्रेण च परिष्वङ्गः संनिपातश्च मैथुने॥ समये च प्रियालाप: स्वयूथ्येषु समुन्नतिः। अभिप्रेतस्य लाभश्च पूजा च जनसंसदि॥
verses.translation: These eight are the creams of rejoicing, O son of Bharata and are obtained this world viz., union with friends, acquirement of immense wealth, embracing a son, intercourse in union, agreeable conversations at proper times, the advancement of the members of one's party, the attainment of desired results and honour and respect among men,

verses.sanskrit: अष्टौ गुणाः पुरुषं दीपयन्ति प्रज्ञा च कौल्यं च दमः श्रुतं च। पराक्रमश्चाबहुभाषिता च दानं यथाशक्ति कृतज्ञता च॥
verses.translation: Eight qualities shed lustre on a man, viz., wisdom, noble blood, Self-restraint, burning prowess, moderation of speech charity to the best of one's own power and gratitude.

verses.sanskrit: नवद्वारमिदं वेश्म त्रिस्थूणं पञ्चसाक्षिकम्। क्षेत्रज्ञाधिष्ठितं विद्वान् यो वेद स परः कविः॥
verses.translation: This house has nine doors, three pillars and five witnesses presided over by the soul. That learned man who knows this is the best of the wise.

verses.sanskrit: दश धर्म न जानन्ति धृतराष्ट्र निबोध तान्। भत्तः प्रमत्त उन्मत्तः श्रान्तः क्रुद्धो बुभुक्षितः॥ त्वरमाणश्च लुब्धश्च भीत: कामी च ते दश। तस्मादेतेषु सर्वेषु न प्रसज्जेत पण्डितः॥
verses.translation: These ten know not what virtue is, o Dhritarashtra, listen (as I name them, the intoxicated, the absent-minded, the insane, the weary, the angry, the hungry, the hasty, the covetous, the frightened and lustful, these are the ten; therefore should a wise man shun the company of these all.

verses.sanskrit: अत्रैवोदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम्। पुत्रार्थमसुरेन्द्रेण गीतं चैव सुधन्वना॥
verses.translation: In this connection people cite the old story that is related about what passed between Sudhanvan and the chief of the Asuras for the sake of his (the latter's) son.

verses.sanskrit: यः काममन्यू प्रजहाति राजा पात्रे प्रतिष्ठापयते धनं च। विशेषविच्छ्रुतवान् क्षिप्रकारी तं सर्वलोकः कुरुते प्रमाणम्॥
verses.translation: The king, who shuns desire and spite and distributes wealth among worthy parties, is discriminating, learned, active and is regarded as an authority by all men.

verses.sanskrit: जानाति विश्वासयितुं मनुष्यान् विज्ञातदोषेषु दधाति दण्डम्। जानाति मात्रां च तथा क्षमा च तं तादृशं श्रीर्जुषते समग्र॥
verses.translation: One who knows how to make other people confident on him, who awards punishment after guilt has been established, knows its proper degree and knows also forgiveness it attended with great prosperity.

verses.sanskrit: सुदुर्बलं नावजानाति कंचिद् युक्तो रिपुं सेवते बुद्धिपूर्वम्। न विचहं रोचयते बलस्थैः काले च यो विक्रमते स धीरः॥
verses.translation: He is a wise man, who does think lightly of the weak (foe), who proceeds with intelligence in respect to a foe waiting for an opportunity, who does not quarrel with the strong and who shows prowess only at the proper time.

verses.sanskrit: दुद्योगमन्विच्छति चाप्रमत्तः। दुःखं च काले सहते महात्मा धुरन्धरस्तस्य जिताः सपत्नाः॥
verses.translation: That illustrious man, who does not grieve when a calamity is already on him and who (then) experts with his senses about him and who also patiently bears calamities (when they are unavoidable), is a noble-minded one and his opponents are always subdued.

verses.sanskrit: अनर्थकं विप्रवासं गृहेभ्यः पापैः सन्धि परदाराभिमर्शम्। दम्भं स्तैन्यं पैशुनं मद्यपानं न सेवते यश्च सुखी सदैव॥
verses.translation: He who never remains away from home for nothing, who does not mix with wicked people, never outrages another's wife, who is not arrogant, who does not steal, is not ungrateful and does not drink, is always happy.

verses.sanskrit: माकारितः शंसति तत्त्वमेव। न मित्रार्थ रोचयते विवाद नापूजितः कुप्यति चाप्यमूढः॥
verses.translation: He who never pursues the three objects (virtue, profit and desire) in a spirit of boastfulness, who when appealed declares the truth, who does not sow discussions even for friends and who through insulted is not angry is the reverse of a fool.

verses.sanskrit: न योऽभ्यसूयत्यनुकम्पते च न दुर्बलः प्रातिभाव्यं करोति। नात्याह किंचित् क्षमते विवादं सर्वत्र तादृग् लभते प्रशंसाम्॥
verses.translation: He, who is not malicious towards others and who being weak does not quarrel, who does not speak haughtily and who always forgives quarrels, wins renown.

verses.sanskrit: यो नोद्धतं कुरुते जातु वेषं न पौरुषेणापि विकत्यतेऽन्यान्। न मूर्छितः कटुकान्याहं किंचित् प्रियं सदा तं कुरुते जनो हि॥
verses.translation: One, who is never haughty, who never speaks ill of others, praising himself and who in a moment of self forgetfulness never speaks harshly, is loved by all men.

verses.sanskrit: न वैरमुद्दीपयति प्रशान्तं न दर्पमारोहति नास्तमेति। न दुर्गतोऽस्मीति करोत्यकार्य तमार्यशीलं परमाहुरार्याः॥
verses.translation: One, who does not revive disputes that have been settled and who behaves not with too much arrogance, nor with excessive humility and who does not do a wrong deed, because he is in distress, is said to be a man, of good behaviour by very good men.

verses.sanskrit: न स्वे सुखे वै कुरुते प्रहर्ष नान्यस्य दुःखे भवति प्रहृष्टः। दत्त्वा न पश्चात् कुरुतेऽनुतापं स कथ्यते सत्पुरुषार्यशीलः॥
verses.translation: One, who exults not in his own happiness nor is giad at another's distress and who does not repent after having given away, is said by good men to be of good conduct.

verses.sanskrit: देशाचारान् समयाञ्जातिधर्मान् बुभूषते यः स परावरज्ञः। स यत्र तत्राभिगतः सदैव महाजनस्याधिपत्यं करोति॥
verses.translation: He, who desires knowledge of the manners and customs of different countries and of the different languages and of the duties of the different casts, knows everything, high and low; wherever he goes, he attains to lordship over great men.

verses.sanskrit: दम्भं मोहं मत्सरं पापकृत्यं राजद्विष्टं पैशुनं पूगवैरम्। मत्तोन्मत्तैर्दुर्जनैश्चापि धादं यः प्रज्ञावान् वर्जयेत् स प्रधानः॥
verses.translation: The wise man who keeps away pride, folly, impertinence, sinful deeds, disloyalty towards his sovereign, roguery enmity with many, speech with the drunk, the insane and the wicked, is a chief among men,

verses.sanskrit: दानं होमं दैवतं मङ्गलानि प्रायश्चित्तान् विविधाँल्लोकवादान्। एतानि यः कुरुते नैत्यकानि तस्योत्थानं देवता राधयन्ति॥
verses.translation: Charity, worship of the gods, auspicious ceremonies and the several penances laid down by men-the gods exert themselves for the advancement of him who practices these daily.

verses.sanskrit: समैर्विवाहं कुरुते न हीनैः समैः सख्यं व्यवहारं कथां च। गुणैविंशोष्टांश्च पुरो दधाति विपश्चितस्तस्य नयाः सुनीताः॥
verses.translation: One, who forms matrimonial relationship with a man in equal station and not with those who are below him and who forms friendships with his equals and converses and treats with them, who puts those that are graced with accomplishments before him-performs acts after the best policy.

verses.sanskrit: मितं भुङ्क्ते संविभज्याश्रितेभ्यो मितं स्वपित्यमितं कर्म कृत्वा। स्तमात्मवन्तं प्रजहत्यनाः॥
verses.translation: One who eats with moderation after doing excessive work, who gives away, (even) to foes when asked, is never beside himself; and evils always avoid him.

verses.sanskrit: चिकीर्षितं विप्रकृतं च यस्य नान्ये जनाः कर्म जानन्ति किंचित्। मन्त्रे गुप्ते सम्यगनुष्ठिते च नाल्पोऽप्यस्थच्यवते कश्चिदर्थः॥
verses.translation: He, whose proposed acts and acts put into execution are seldom known to others and whose intentions are kept secret and put into practice suitably, does not fail in his objects even when trifling.

verses.sanskrit: यः सर्वभूतप्रशमे निविष्टः सत्यो मृदुर्मानकृच्छुद्धभावः। अतीव स ज्ञायते ज्ञातिमध्ये महामणिर्जात्य इव प्रसन्नः॥
verses.translation: He, who is engaged in doing good to all creatures, who is attached to truth, not haughty, of a good mind and not a miser, is known very well among his cousins like a gem of the purest ray obtained from a good mine.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मनापनपते भृशं नरः स सर्वलोकस्य गुरुर्भवत्युता अनन्ततेजा: सुमनाः समाहितः स तेजसा सूर्य इवावभासते॥
verses.translation: The man, who is ashamed of his faults unknown to others, is respected by all men, being of illimitable luster, of an excellent mind; and his mind is intent on his inner self and his luster shines like the sun.

verses.sanskrit: वने जाता: शापदग्धस्य राज्ञः पाण्डोः पुत्राः पञ्च पञ्चेन्द्रकल्पाः। त्वयैव बाला वर्धिताः शिक्षिताश्च तवादेशं पालयन्त्याम्बिकेय॥
verses.translation: The king Pandu, burning under the influence of a curse, begot in the woods, five sons each equal to Indra; under your supervision did the boys grow up and were instructed and you have been rearing them up to now, O son of Ambika.

verses.sanskrit: प्रदायैषामुचितं तात राज्यं सुखी पुत्रैः सहितो मोदमानः। न देवानां नापि च मानुषाणां भविष्यसि त्वं तर्कणीयो नरेन्द्र॥
verses.translation: Giving back to them their rightful kingdom, O Sire, be happy with your sons and pass your time agreeably; O Lord of man, then will you not be mistrusted by the gods, nor by the men.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच जाग्रतो दह्यमानस्य यत् कार्यमनुपश्यसि। तद् ब्रूहि त्वं हि नस्तात धर्मार्थकुशलो ह्यसि॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said Tell me what you think ought to be done by a sleepless man and burning (with anxiety); you alone among us, are accomplished, both in the codes of morality and policy.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं मां यथावदं विदुर प्रशाधिं प्रज्ञापूर्वं सर्वमजातशत्रोः। यन्मन्यसे पथ्यमदीनसत्त्व श्रेयस्करं ब्रूहि तद् वै कुरूणाम्॥
verses.translation: Tell me, O Vidura, as suits the occasion, after exercising your wisdom, all that you consider to be beneficial for Ajatashatru; tell speak also what conduces to the good of the Kurus.

verses.sanskrit: पापाशङ्की पापमेवानुपश्यन् पृच्छामि त्वां व्याकुलेनात्मनाहम्। न्मनीषितं सर्वमजातशत्रोः॥
verses.translation: Committing sin and looking back on my misdeeds, I ask you with anxious heart, O wise one, all that is the mind of Ajatashatru.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच शुभं वा यदि वा पापं द्वेष्यं वा यदि वा प्रियम्। अपृष्टस्तस्य तद् ब्रूयाद् यस्य नेच्छेत् पराभवम्॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Good or bad, agreeable or disagreeable should one speak out, though unasked, to one whose downfall he dose not wish.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् वक्ष्यामि ते राजन् हितं यत् स्यात् कुरून् प्रति। वचः श्रेयस्करं धयं ब्रुवतस्तन्निबोध मे॥
verses.translation: Therefore shall I say to you, O king, what is good for the Kurus, listen to me, (while I am) speaking words that are conducive to your interests and consistent with morality.

verses.sanskrit: मिथ्योपेतानि कर्माणि सिद्धेयुर्यानि भारत। अनुपायप्रयुक्तानि मा स्म तेषु मनः कृथाः॥
verses.translation: The misdeeds, that are attainable only by dishonest means, do not set your mind on, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: तथैव योगविहितं यत्तु तु कर्म न सिध्यति। उपाययुक्तं मेधावी न तत्र ग्लपयेन्मनः॥
verses.translation: If an object cannot be attained, O king, even with-proper means, an intelligent man dose not distress his mind about it.

verses.sanskrit: अनुबन्धानपेक्षेत सानुबन्धेषु कर्मसु। सम्प्रधार्य च कुर्वीत न वेगेन समाचरेत्॥
verses.translation: The reasons of an act and its result should be carefully considered before it is done without due deliberation.

verses.sanskrit: अनुबन्धं च सम्प्रेक्ष्य विपाकं चैव कर्मणाम्। उत्थानमात्मनश्चैव धीरः कुर्वीत वा न वा॥
verses.translation: A wise man dose or dose not do an act after reflecting on the reasons of act and its results if done, as also the energy of his own self.

verses.sanskrit: यः प्रमाणं न जानाति स्थाने वृद्धौ तथा क्षये। कोशे जनपदे दण्डे न स राज्येऽवतिष्ठते॥
verses.translation: One (a king) who dose not know the measure of his territory, population and punishment, continue in (the enjoyment of) his kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: यस्त्वेतनि प्रमाणानि यथोक्तान्यनुपश्यति। युक्तो धर्मार्थवोर्ज्ञाने स राज्यमधिगच्छति॥
verses.translation: He who knows these prescribed in books) is versed in the sciene of morality and earthly good and retains his kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: न राज्यं प्राप्तमित्येव वर्तितव्यमसाम्प्रतम्। श्रियं ह्यविनयो हन्ति जरा रूपमिवोत्तम्॥
verses.translation: One (a king) should not live a haughty life considering that he was already obtained a kingdom, for haughtiness destroys kingly prosperity, as old age (destroys) good appearance. measures as

verses.sanskrit: भक्ष्योत्तमप्रतिच्छन्नं मत्स्यो बडिशमायसम्। लोभाभिपाति ग्रसते नानुबन्धमवेक्षते॥
verses.translation: A fish out of greediness dose not think about the result of an action and swallows up the iron hook concealed in a dainty morsel.

verses.sanskrit: यच्छक्यं ग्रसितुं चस्यं चस्तं परिणमेच्च यत्। हितं च परिणामे यत् तदाद्यं भूतिमिच्छता॥
verses.translation: One who desire prosperity, should swallow only that which can be swallowed and which, being swallowed, can be digested and may contribute to benefit in the end.

verses.sanskrit: वनस्पतेरपक्वानि फलानि प्रचिनोति यः। स नाप्नोति रसं तेभ्यो बीजं चास्य विनश्यति॥
verses.translation: He who plucks unripe fruits from tress, dose not get juice out of it; and moreover he destroys the seed.

verses.sanskrit: यस्तु पक्वमुपादत्ते काले परिणतं फलम्। फलाद् रसं स लभते बीजाच्चैव फलं पुनः॥
verses.translation: And he, who gets ripe fruit in the proper season, gets the juice of the fruits well as other fruits reproduced from the seeds.

verses.sanskrit: यथा मधु समादत्ते रक्षन् पुष्पाणि षट्पदः। तद्वदर्थान् मनुष्येभ्य आदद्यादविहिंसया॥
verses.translation: As the bees suck honey without injuring the flowers, so should wealth be taken from men (by king) without injuring them.

verses.sanskrit: पुष्पं पुष्पं विचिन्वीत मूलच्छेदं न कारयेत्। मालाकार इवारामे न यथाऽङ्गारकारकः॥
verses.translation: Carefully plucking the flowers, one should not hurt the roots of the plants, like a maker of garlands in garden in a garden and not like a seller of charcoal.

verses.sanskrit: केन्नु मे स्यादिदं कृत्वा किनु मे स्यादकुर्वतः। इति कर्माणि संचिन्त्य कुर्याद् वा पुरुषो न वा॥
verses.translation: Having carefully considered what will befall me after doing an act or not doing it, a man should do things or not do them.

verses.sanskrit: अनारभ्या भवन्त्याः केचिन्नित्यं तथागताः। कृतः पुरुषकारो हि भवेद् येषु निरर्थकः॥
verses.translation: Acts should not be commenced, which cannot be done for certain and which, if commenced, render the exertions of a man fruitless.

verses.sanskrit: प्रसादो निष्फलो यस्य क्रोधश्चापि निरर्थकः। न तं भर्तारमिच्छन्ति षण्डं पतिमिव स्त्रियः॥
verses.translation: Whose favour is useless and whose ire is impotent, the people do not wish that sort of man for a lord; as a woman dose not wise an impotent man to be her husband.

verses.sanskrit: कांश्चिदर्थान् नरः प्राज्ञोलघुमूलान् महाफलान्। क्षिप्रमारभते कर्तुं न विघ्नयति तादृशान्॥
verses.translation: A wise man dose not wait, but quickly commences doing such acts, as involve little labour but produce great results.

verses.sanskrit: ऋजु पश्यति यः सर्वं चक्षुषानुपिबन्निव। आसीनमपि तूष्णीकमनुरज्यन्ति तं प्रजाः॥
verses.translation: He (the king) who looks affectionately, as if drinking with his eyes, on all, though only sitting without any exertion-can inspire affection in all his subjects.

verses.sanskrit: सुपुष्पितः स्यादफल: फलित: स्याद् दुरारुहः। अपक्तः पक्वसंकाशो न तु शीर्येत कर्हिचित्॥
verses.translation: If a tree is full of blossoms though the king look affectionately on this let it not be fruitful; and if it is fruitful let the tree be inaccessible; and if the fruits are unripe let them appear as ripe. A king, who acts thus, is never weakened.

verses.sanskrit: चक्षुषा मनसा वाचा कर्मणा च चतुर्विधम्। प्रसादयति यो लोकं लोकोऽनुप्रसीदति॥
verses.translation: Society favours him, who pleases all in four ways viz., by the eyes, by the mind, by words and by the act.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मात् त्रस्यन्ति भूतानि मृगव्याधान्मृगा इव। सागरान्तामपि महीं लब्वा स परिहीयते॥
verses.translation: He, whom all creatures dread as deer fear the hunter, loses it in the end even after acquiring the earth (for his kingdom) having the sea round it.

verses.sanskrit: पितृपैतामहं राज्यं प्राप्तवान् स्वेन कर्मणा। वायुरभ्रमिवासाद्य भ्रंशयत्यनये स्थितः॥
verses.translation: He (the king), who is addicted to unfairness, destroys by his own acts the kingdom inherited from his father and grandfather; even as the wind scatters away the clothing them.

verses.sanskrit: धर्ममाचरतो राज्ञः सद्धिश्चरितमादितः। वसुधा वसुसम्पूर्णा वर्धते भूतिवर्धिनी॥
verses.translation: The earth full of wealth increases the worldly prosperity of the king who practices virtue followed by good men from the ancient time.

verses.sanskrit: अथ संत्यजतो धर्ममधर्मं चानुतिष्ठतः। प्रतिसंवेष्टते भूमिरग्नौ चर्माहितं यथा।॥
verses.translation: Again, the territories, that of king who leaving virtue practices unrighteousness, contract like a piece of leather thrown into the fire.

verses.sanskrit: य एव यत्नः क्रियते परराष्ट्रविमर्दने। स एव यत्नः कर्तव्यः स्वराष्ट्रपरिपालने॥
verses.translation: The care, that is bestowed on despoiling another of his kingdom, should be spent in protecting one's own kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मेण राज्यं विन्देत धर्मेण परिपालयेत्। धर्ममूलां श्रियं प्राप्य न जहाति न हीयते॥
verses.translation: By means of virtue should a kingdom be attained; and by means of virtue should it be governed. The kingly prosperity, that has virtue for its basis, is never lost, nor it flies away.

verses.sanskrit: अप्युन्मत्तात् प्रलपतो बालाच्च परिजल्पतः। सर्वतः सारमादद्यादश्मभ्य इव काञ्चनम्॥
verses.translation: Enlightenment should be sought for from everything-even from the leavings of a lunatic and the prattles of a child, as gold from stones.

verses.sanskrit: सुव्याहृतानि सूक्तानि सुकृतानि ततस्ततः। संचिन्वन् धीर आसीत शिलाहारी शिलं यथा।॥
verses.translation: A wise man should live, picking out good manners, good sayings and good deeds, even as one given up to the Shila mode of life picks grains of corn from the field.

verses.sanskrit: गन्धेन गावः पश्यन्ति वेदैः पश्यन्ति ब्राह्मणाः। चारैः पश्यन्ति राजानश्चक्षुर्ष्यामितरे जनाः॥
verses.translation: King see by means of smell, Brahmanas see by means of the Vedas, kings see by means of scouts and other people through eyes.

verses.sanskrit: भूयांसं लभते क्लेशं या गौर्भवति दुर्दुहा। अथ या सुदुहा राजन् नैव तां वितुदन्त्यपि॥
verses.translation: The cow that is difficult to milk gets great trouble; but one that is easy to milk, O king, gets nothing.

verses.sanskrit: यदतप्तं प्रणमति न तत् संतापयन्त्यपि। यच्च स्वयं नतं दारु न तत् सन्तापयन्त्यपि॥
verses.translation: That which bends without being heated is not heated at all; the wood that bends of itself is never heated.

verses.sanskrit: एतयोपमया धीरः संनमेत बलीयसे। इन्द्राय स प्रणमते नमते यो बलीयसे॥
verses.translation: Following this example, a wise man should bend to one stronger than himself and he who bends before the stronger bows down to Indra himself.

verses.sanskrit: पर्जन्यनाथाः पशवो राजानो मन्त्रिवान्धवाः। पतयो बान्धवाः स्त्रीणां ब्राह्मणा वेदबान्धवाः॥
verses.translation: Creatures depend on clouds; kings have the counsellors for their friends; husbands are the friends of women and the Brahmanas have the Vedas for their friends.

verses.sanskrit: सत्येन रक्ष्यते धर्मो विद्या योगेन रक्ष्यते। मृजया रक्ष्यते रूपं कुलं वृत्तेन रक्ष्यते॥
verses.translation: Virtue is preserved by truthfulness; learning is preserved by exercise; beauty is preserved by toilet; and noble birth is preserved by good manners.

verses.sanskrit: मानेन रक्ष्यते धान्यमश्वान् रक्षत्यनुक्रमः। अभीक्ष्णदर्शनं गाश्च स्त्रियो रक्ष्याः कुचैलतः।। :॥
verses.translation: Corn is preserves by measure exercise preserves steeds; strict and constant supervision preserves horses; and women is preserved by ragged garments.

verses.sanskrit: न कुलं वृत्तहीनस्य प्रमाणमिति मे मतिः। अन्तेष्वपि हि जातानां वृत्तमेव विशिष्यते॥
verses.translation: It is my opinion that noble birth in one who is not of good behaviours dose not mean virtue; and that good manners in one born low should command respect.

verses.sanskrit: य ईर्षुः परवित्तेषु रूपे वीर्ये कुलान्वये। सुखसौभाग्यसत्कारे तस्य व्याधिरनन्तकः॥
verses.translation: He who is envious of other people's wealth, beauty, prowess, good birth, happiness, luck and reward, suffers a disease which has no cure.

verses.sanskrit: अकार्यकरणाद् भीतः कार्याणां च विवर्जनात्। अकाले मन्त्रभेदाच्च येन मायेन्न तत् पिबेत्॥
verses.translation: He by whom is feared the doing of improper acts or the omission of proper acts or the premature disclosure of his intentions, should not drink that which inebriates.

verses.sanskrit: विद्यामदो धनमदस्तृतीयोऽभिजनो मदः। मदा एतेऽवलिप्तानामेत एव सतां दमाः॥
verses.translation: Pride of learning, pride of wealth, pride of connections the pride in these the good people should restrain.

verses.sanskrit: तावन तस्य सुकृतं किंचित्कार्ये कदाचन। मन्यन्ते सन्तमात्मानमसन्तमपि विश्रुतम्॥
verses.translation: Bad people, asked by honest ones to do something for them, consider themselves as honest after doing very doing very little of that, even though they are well-known to be dishonest.

verses.sanskrit: गतिरात्मवतां सन्तः सन्त एवं सतां गतिः। असतां च गतिः सन्तो न त्वसन्तः सतां गतिः॥
verses.translation: The righteous are their own refuge and that of other righteous men. The righteous, too are the refuge of the unrighteous; and the unrighteous are never the refuge of the righteous.

verses.sanskrit: जिता सभा वस्त्रवता मिष्टाशा गोमता जिता। अध्वा जितो यानवता सर्वं शीलवता जितम्॥
verses.translation: In (a debate in) an assembly, he who is well dressed, comes off victorious, he who is owner of king triumphs over the desire to eat sweets; and the road is triumphed over by one who has conveyances; and everything is subjugated by one of good behaviour.

verses.sanskrit: शीलं प्रधानं पुरुषे तद् यस्येह प्रणश्यति। न तस्य जीवितेनार्थो न धनेन न बन्धुभिः॥
verses.translation: Good manner is the prime thing in a man; and he that has not got it in this life gains nothing by life, by wealth or by friends.

verses.sanskrit: आढ्यानां मांसपरमं मध्यानां गोरसोत्तरम्। तैलोत्तरं दरिद्राणां भोजनं भरतर्षभ॥
verses.translation: O bull of the race of Bharata, flesh is the chief food of the rich, milk that of the middle classes and oil that of the poor.

verses.sanskrit: सम्पन्नतरमेवान्नं दरिद्रा भुञ्जते सदा। क्षुत् स्वादुतां जनयति सा चाढ्येषु सुदुर्लभा॥
verses.translation: The poor however ever take the most delicious food; for hunger imparts sweetness of taste to it; it (hunger) is ever rare among the opulent.

verses.sanskrit: प्रायेण श्रीमतां लोके भोक्तुं शक्तिर्न विद्यते। जीर्यन्त्यपि हि काष्ठानि दरिद्राणां महीपते॥
verses.translation: In this, world the opulent mostly have no capacity, for eating, whiled O Lord of the earth, pieces of wood are digested by the poor.

verses.sanskrit: अवृत्तिर्भयमन्त्यानां मध्यानां मरणाद् भयम्। उत्तमानां तु मानामवमानात् परं भयम्॥
verses.translation: Loss of livelihood is feared by men of the lower classes; death is feared by the middle classes and insult is greatly feared by good men.

verses.sanskrit: ऐश्वर्यमदपापिष्ठा मदाः पानमदादयः। ऐश्वर्यमदमत्तो हि नापतित्वा विबुध्यते॥
verses.translation: The devils who are proud of their wealth are worse than those intoxicated with wine; for he, who is intoxicated with the pride of wealth, is not brought to his senses unless he meet with the reverse.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियैरिन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तमानैरनिग्रहैः। तैरयं ताप्यते लोको नक्षत्राणि चहैरिवा॥
verses.translation: This world is affected by the senses directed to their own objects without any control in the same was as stars (are affected by the planets).

verses.sanskrit: यो जितः पञ्चवर्गेण सहजेनात्मकर्षिणा। आपदस्तस्य वर्धन्ते शुक्लपक्ष इवोडुराट्॥
verses.translation: In the life of one, who is subjugated by the five senses in their natural state ever impelling him towards action, calamities ever increase like the moon after the new moon.

verses.sanskrit: अविजित्य य आत्मानममात्यान् विजिगीषते। अमित्रान् वाजितामात्यः सोऽवशः परिहीयते॥
verses.translation: He who desires to bring his advisers under control without controlling himself, who desires to control his enemies without controlling his advisers, at last yields, deprived of strength.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानमेव प्रथमं द्वेष्यरूपेण यो जयेत्। ततोऽमात्यानमित्रांश्च न मोघं विजिगीषते॥
verses.translation: He, therefore, who brings himself first under control thinking that his senses are his prime enemies, in the end subjugates as a matter of certainty, his advisers and his enemies.

verses.sanskrit: वश्येन्द्रियं जितात्मानं धृतदण्डं विकारिषु। परीक्ष्य कारिणं धीरमत्यन्तं धीरमत्यन्तं श्रीनिषेवते।।५८
verses.translation: Great prosperity comes up on him who has controlled his senses or subjugated himself and who can hold the rod (of punishment) against all offenders without partiality and who acts with circumspection and who is patient.

verses.sanskrit: त्रात्मा नियन्तेन्द्रियाण्यस्य चाश्वाः। र्दान्तः सुखं याति रथीव धीरः॥
verses.translation: The body of a man, O king, is like the car; the soul, the driver; and the senses, the horses, Drawn by those excellent steeds when well trained, he that is wise and patient performs the journey in peace.

verses.sanskrit: एतान्यनिगृहीतानि व्यापादयितुमप्यलम्। अविधेया इवादान्ता हयाः पथि कुसारथिम्॥
verses.translation: These (the senses) when untrained lead one to destruction; in the same way the untrained horses lead the unskillful drivers (to destruction.)

verses.sanskrit: अनर्थमर्थतः पश्यन्नर्थ चैवाप्यनर्थतः। इन्द्रियैरजितैर्बाल: सुदुः :खं मन्यते सुखम्॥
verses.translation: The inexperienced man, who wants to select evil from good and good from evil with the aid of his senses which he has not mastered, considers great misery to be happiness.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मार्थो यः परित्यज्य स्यादिन्द्रियवशानुगः। श्रीप्राणधनदारेभ्यः क्षिप्रं स परिहीयते॥
verses.translation: He, who having forsaken both virtue and worldly gains, the follows, the lead of his senses, very soon comes to lose prosperity, life wealth and wife.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थानामीश्वरो यः स्यादिन्द्रियाणामनीश्वरः। इन्द्रियाणामनैश्वर्यादैश्वर्याद् भ्रश्यते हि सः॥
verses.translation: The lord of riches, who is a slave of his senses, loses his riches through his want of control over the senses.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मनाऽऽत्मानमन्विच्छेन्मनोबुद्धीन्द्रियैर्यतैः। आत्मा ह्येवात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
verses.translation: A man should try to see and know nimself by mastering his mind, intelligence and senses; for, he himself is his own friend and himself is his enemy.

verses.sanskrit: बन्धुरात्माऽऽत्मनस्तस्य येनैवात्माऽऽत्मना जितः। स एव नियतो बन्धुः स एव नियतो रिपुः॥
verses.translation: He, by whom self has been subjugated by self and has himself for his friend; for himself is ever his friend and himself is ever his enemy.

verses.sanskrit: क्षुद्राक्षेणेव जालेन झपावपिहितावुरू। कामश्च राजन् क्रोधश्च तौ प्रज्ञानं विलुम्पतः॥
verses.translation: In the same way, as a big fish breaks through a net of thin chords, so, O king, desire and anger cast wisdom in the shade.

verses.sanskrit: समवेक्ष्येह धर्मार्थो सम्भारान् योऽधिगच्छति। स वै सम्भृतसम्भारः सततः सुखमेधते॥
verses.translation: He, who having paid due regard to virtue and worldly gains seeks the acquirement of success, gets what he wants and ever is in happiness.

verses.sanskrit: यः पञ्चाभ्यन्तराञ्छत्रूनविजित्य मनोमयान्। जिगीषति रिपूनन्यान् रिपवोऽभिभवन्ति तम्॥
verses.translation: He, who without subjugating the five enemies within that have their origin in the mind, desires to subdue other enemies, is vanquished by these enemies.

verses.sanskrit: दृश्यन्ते हि महात्मानो बध्यमानाः स्वकर्मभिः। इन्द्रियाणामनीशत्वाद् राजानो राज्यविभ्रमैः॥
verses.translation: Instances are seen in which noble hearted kings, out of lust of territory, are destroyed by acts sorely through the want of control over their senses.

verses.sanskrit: स्तुल्यो दण्डः स्पृशते मिश्रभावात्। शुष्केणार्दै दह्यते मिश्रभावात् तस्मात् पापैः सह सन्धिं न कुर्यात्॥
verses.translation: Equal punishment overtakes the sinless with the sinful, when these two constantly associate; even as the wet fuel burns with the dry. Therefore friendship should not be established with the sinful.

verses.sanskrit: निजानुत्पततः शत्रून् पञ्च पञ्चप्रयोजनान्। यो मोहान निगृह्णाति तमापद् ग्रसते नरम्॥
verses.translation: Misery overtakes the man, who dose not subdue his five soaring foes out of ignorance which have five different objects.

verses.sanskrit: अनसूयाऽऽर्जवं शौचं संतोषः प्रियवादिता। दमः सत्यमनायासो न भवन्ति दुरात्मनाम्॥
verses.translation: Guilelessness, simplicity, sanctity. contentment, sweetness of speech, self-control, truthfulness and steadiness are never the attributes of the wicked.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मज्ञानमनायासीत्ततिक्षा धर्मनित्यता। वाक् चैव गुप्ता दानं च नैतान्यन्त्येषु भारत॥
verses.translation: Spiritual knowledge steadiness, patience, constancy in virtue, secret counsels and charity-these are not to be found in men of the lower, Odescendant of Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: आक्रोशपरिवादाभ्यां विहिंसन्त्यबुधा वुधान्। वक्ता पापमुपादत्ते क्षममाणो विमुच्यते॥
verses.translation: The ignorant seek to injure the wise by malice back-biting; and the speaker takes upon himself the load of his (wise man's) sins, which he (the wise man) casts off by forgiving the ignorant.

verses.sanskrit: हिंसा बलमसाधूनां राज्ञां दण्डविधिर्बलम्। शुश्रूषा तु बलं स्त्रीणां क्षमा गुणवतां बलम्॥
verses.translation: Malice is the strength of the unrighteous; the penal code is the strength of the kings; ministration to the sick is the strength of women; and forgiveness is the strength of the virtuous.

verses.sanskrit: वाक्संयमो हि नृपते सुदुष्करतमो मतः। अर्थवच्च विचित्रं च न शक्यं बहु भाषितुम्॥
verses.translation: The control over speech, O lord of men, is thought to be most difficult; and it is not possible to speak much full of meaning in an entertaining way.

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यावहति कल्याणं विविधं वाक् सुभाषिता। सैव दुर्भाषिता राजन्ननायोपपद्यते॥
verses.translation: Words spoken sweetly bring on several of the blessings; and the same (words) spoken harshly, O king generate evils.

verses.sanskrit: रोहते सायकैर्विद्धं वनं परशुना हतम्। वाचा दुरुक्तं बीभत्सं न संरोहति वाक्क्षतम्॥
verses.translation: A forest, pierced by arrows or cut down by scythes, grows again; but the heart pierced with words, harsh and rude, never recovers.

verses.sanskrit: कर्णिनालीकनाराचान् निर्हरन्ति शरीरतः। वाक्शल्यस्तु न निर्हर्तुं शक्यो हृदिशयोहिसः॥
verses.translation: Arrows and darts can be extracted from the body; but the darts of words cannot be extracted from the depth of the heart.

verses.sanskrit: वाक्सायका वदनानिष्पतन्ति यैराहतः शोचति रात्र्यहानि। परस्य नामर्मसु ते पतन्ति तान् पण्डितो नावसृजेत् परेभ्यः॥
verses.translation: Arrows of words are shot from the mouth, wounded by which one grieves night and day; for they touch the innermost recesses of the hearts of others? Therefore a wise man should not fling on others.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मै देवाः प्रयच्छन्ति पुरुषाय पराभवम्। बुद्धि तस्यापकर्षन्ति सोऽवाचीनानि पश्यति॥
verses.translation: That man, to whom defeat has been sent by the gods, has his senses lost; and, therefore, he does stoop to mean acts.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धौ कलुषभूतायां विनाशे प्रत्युपस्थिते। अनयो नयसंकाशो हृदयानापसर्पति॥
verses.translation: On intellect becoming dim and on the approach of ruin, wrong, in the disguise of right, dose not remove from the mind.

verses.sanskrit: सेयं बुद्धिः परीता ते पुत्राणां भरतर्षभ। पाण्डवानां विरोधेन न चैनानवबुध्यसे॥
verses.translation: The dim intellect has overpowered your son, O bull of Bharata race; same now you do not clearly see it owing to your enmity against the Pandavas.

verses.sanskrit: राजा लक्षणसम्पन्नस्त्रैलोक्यस्यापि यो भवेत्। शिष्यस्ते शासिता सोऽस्तु धृतराष्ट्रा युधिष्ठिरः॥
verses.translation: A king with auspicious marks and the ruler of the three worlds, Yudhishthira waits on your commands, O Dhritarashtra; let him be the ruler of the earth.

verses.sanskrit: अतीत्य सर्वान् पुत्रांस्ते भागधेयपुरस्कृतः। तेजसा प्रज्ञया चैव युक्तो धर्मार्थतत्त्ववित्॥ अनुक्रोशादानृशंस्याद् योऽसौ धर्मभृतां वरः। गौरवात् तव राजेन्द्र बहून् क्लेशांस्तितिक्षति॥
verses.translation: Endue with good qualities; he is, to the exclusion of all your sons, the fore-most among your heirs; he is endued with energy and wisdom and versed both in the codes of morality and earthly good. Out of kindness and simplicity that chief among virtuous men has patiently borne many a trouble in order to uphold your story.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ब्रूहि भूयो महाबुद्ध धर्मार्थसहितं वचः। शृण्वतो नास्ति मे तृप्तिर्विचित्राणीह भाषसे॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said O you of great wisdom, repeat these sayings, that are consist on with morality and worldly benefit. What you say is most interesting; my desire for hearing them has not been satisfied.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच सर्वतीर्थेषु वा स्नानं सर्वभूतेषु चार्जवम्। उभे त्वेते समे स्यातामार्जवं वा विशिष्यते॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Bathing in all the holy places and kindness to all beings-both these are equal. Perhaps kindness is better.

verses.sanskrit: आर्जवं प्रतिपद्यस्व पुत्रेषु सततं विभो। इह कीर्तिं परां प्राप्य प्रेत्य स्वर्गमवाप्स्यसि॥
verses.translation: O Lord, always show kindness to your sons; and thus you will attain to heaven after having gained great fame in this world.

verses.sanskrit: यावत् कीर्तिर्मनुष्यस्य पुण्या लोके प्रगीयते। तावत् स पुरुषव्याघ्र स्वर्गलोके महीयते॥
verses.translation: As long as a man's deeds are spoken of in this world, so long, O foremost among men, he is glorious in heaven.

verses.sanskrit: अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम्। विरोचनस्य संवादं केशिन्यर्थं सुधन्वना॥
verses.translation: As an instance of this is cited the old story about the conversation between Virochana and Sudhanvan, suitors of Keshini's hand.

verses.sanskrit: स्वयंवरे स्थिता कन्या केशिनी नाम नामतः। रूपेणाप्रतिमा राजन् विशिष्टपतिकाम्यया।॥
verses.translation: A maiden named Keshini, of peerless beauty, o king, with the desire of a good husband, resolved to to choose in Svayamvara.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचनोऽथ दैतेयस्तदा तत्राजगाम ह। प्राप्तुमिच्छंस्ततस्तत्र दैत्येन्द्रं प्राह केशिनी॥
verses.translation: Then Virochana, a son of Diti, came there with the desire of winning her. Then did Keshini address that chief among the Daityas thus. one a

verses.sanskrit: केशिन्युवाच किं ब्राह्मणा: स्विच्छ्रेयांसो दितिजाः स्विद् विरोचन। अथ केन स्म पर्यवं सुधन्वा नाधिरोहति॥
verses.translation: Keshini said Are the Brahmanas superior or are the sons of Diti superior, O Virochana? why should not Sudhanvan sit on the sofa?

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन उवाच प्राजापत्यास्तु वै श्रेष्ठा वयं केशिनि सत्तमाः। अस्माकं खल्विमे लोकाः के देवाः के द्विजातयः॥
verses.translation: Virochana said We, the descendants of Prajapati, O Keshini, are the best among creatures. This world is ours. Who are the gods and who are the twice-born person?

verses.sanskrit: केशिन्युवाच इहैवावां प्रतीक्षाव उपस्थाने विरोचन। सुधन्वा प्रातरागन्ता पश्येयं वां समागतौ॥
verses.translation: Keshini said Even in this very Pavilion shall we wait, O Virochana. Sudhanvan will come in the morning to-morrow; I shall both of you together.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन उवाच तथा भद्रे करिष्यामि यथा त्वं भीरु भाषसे। सुधन्वानं च मां चैव प्रातर्दृष्टासि संगतौ॥
verses.translation: Virochana said 0gentle maiden, I shall do as you say, 0 timid one; you will see in the morning myself and Sudhanvan together.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच अतीतायां च शर्वर्यामुदिते सूर्यमण्डले। अथाजगाम तं देशं सुधन्वा राजसत्तम। विरोचनो यत्र विभो केशिन्या सहितः स्थितः॥
verses.translation: Vidura said The night had passed away and the solar disc had appeared, there came to that country, O best kings, Sudhanvan, where, O Lord, Virochana was staying with Keshini.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वा च समागच्छत् प्राहादि केशिनी तथा। समागतं द्विजं दृष्ट्वा केशिनी भरतर्षभ। प्रत्युत्थायासनं तस्मै पाद्यमयं तदौ पुनः॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan came to the son of Prahrada and Keshini; the latter, O best among the race if Bharata, having observed the approach of a Brahmana, rose up; and gave him water to wash his feet and the Arghya (mark of respect).

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच अन्वालभे हिरण्मयं प्राह्रादे ते वरासनम्। एकत्वमुपसम्पन्नो न त्वासेऽहं त्वया सह॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said (When asked by Virochana to sit on the sofa he occupied) O son of Prahrada, how can I touch that excellent gold seat occupied by you? I shall then come down to the same level with yourself. I shall not sit with you.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन उवाच तवाहते तु फलकं कूर्च वाप्यथवा बृसी। सुधन्वन्न त्वमोऽसि मया सह समासनम्॥
verses.translation: Virochana said You are fit for (sitting on) a plank, a skin or a mat. O Sudhanvan, you are not fit for a seat equal to mine.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच पितापुत्रौ सहासीतां द्वौ विप्रौ क्षत्रियावपि। वृद्धौ वैश्यौ च शूद्रौ च न त्वन्यावितरेतरम्॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said Father and son, two Brahmanas, two Kshatriyas, two old men and two Sudras can sit together. Excepting these, no others can sit together.

verses.sanskrit: पिता हि ते समासीनमुपासीतैव मामधः। बालः सुखैधितो गेहे न त्वं किंचन बुध्यसे॥
verses.translation: Your father used to respect me and occupy a seat lower than mine. You are a child bred in luxury at home; and do not know anything.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन उवाच हिरण्यं च गवावं च यद् वित्तमसुरेषु नः। सुधन्वन् विपणे तेन प्रश्नं पृच्छाव ये विदुः॥
verses.translation: Virochana said The gold kine, horses and all other wealth that we Asuras have-staking all these, o Sudhanvan, let us ask this question to them that know.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच हिरण्यं च गवाश्चं च तवैवास्तु विरोचना प्राणयोस्तु पणं कृत्वा प्रश्नं पृच्छाव ये विदुः॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said O Virochana, let alone your gold, kine and horses. Let us stake our very lives and ask them the question that are able to answer.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन उवाच आवां कुत्र गमिष्यावः प्राणयोविपणे कृते। न तु देवेष्वहं स्थाता न मनुष्येषु कहिचित्॥
verses.translation: Virochana said Where shall we go after staking our lives. I shall not stand before any of the gods and never before any among men.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच पितरं ते गमिष्यावः प्राणयोर्विपणे कृते। पुत्रस्यापि स हेतोहि प्रह्लाद नानृतं वदेत्॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said We shall go your father after staking our lives. Prahrada will not tell a lie even for the sake of his son.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच एवं कृतपणौ क्रुद्धौ तत्राभिजग्मतुस्तदा। विरोचनसुधन्वानौ प्रह्लाद यत्र तिष्ठति॥
verses.translation: Vidura said In this way having laid wagers, Virochana and Sudhanvan, enraged at each other, then went to the place where Prahrada was staying.

verses.sanskrit: प्रह्लाद उवाच इमौ तौ सम्प्रदृश्येते याभ्यां न चरितं सह। आशीविषाविव क्रुद्धावेकमार्गाविहागतौ॥
verses.translation: Prahrada said These two now appear, who have never before been together, like two enraged serpents coming by the same road.

verses.sanskrit: किं वै सहैवं चरथो न पुरा चरथः सह। विरोचनैतत् पृच्छामि किं ते सख्यं सुधन्वना॥
verses.translation: Is it that friendship has now been established between you, among whom these was no friendship before? O Virochana, I ask you why this friendship with Sudhanvan.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन उवाच न मे सुधन्वना सख्यं प्राणयोर्विपणावहे। प्रह्लाद तत्त्वं पृच्छामि मा प्रश्नमनृतं वदेः॥
verses.translation: Virochana said There is no friendship between myself and Sudhanvan. (the Truth is that) we have wagered our lives. O sire, I shall ask you a question; do not answer it untruely.

verses.sanskrit: प्रह्लाद उवाच उदकं मधुपर्क वाप्यानयन्तु सुधन्वने। ब्रह्मन्नभ्यर्चनीयोऽसि श्वेता गौः पीवरी कृता।॥
verses.translation: Prahrada said Let water, honey and curds be brought for Sudhanvan. O Brahmana, you ought to be respected by ine. A white and healthy cow is ready for you.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच उदकं मधुपर्कं च पथिष्वेवार्पितं मम। प्रह्लाद त्वं तु मे तथ्यं प्रश्न प्रबूहि पृच्छतः। किं ब्राह्मणाः स्विच्छ्रेयांस उताहो स्विद् विरोचनः।।
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said Water, honey and curds have been presented to me on the way. O Prahrada, I ask you a question; answer truly what I ask you. Are the Brahmanas superior or is Virochana superior?

verses.sanskrit: प्रह्लाद उवाच पुत्र एको मम ब्रह्मंस्त्वं च साक्षादिहास्थितः। तयोर्विवदतोः प्रश्नं कथमस्मद्विधो वदेत्॥
verses.translation: Prahrada said I have got only one son; and you are a Brahmana present here in person. How can one situated as I am, answer the question, which is a matter of dispute between yourselves.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच गां प्रदद्यास्त्वौरसाय यद्वान्यत् स्यात् प्रियं धनम्। द्वयोर्विवदतोस्तथ्यं वाच्यं च मतिमंस्वया॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said Keep your cow and whatever wealth you hold dear for yourself; but, O wise man, you should speak the truth in a matter, on which we two are disputing.

verses.sanskrit: प्रह्लाद उवाच अथ यो नैव प्रब्रूयात् सत्यं वा यदि वानृतम्। एतत् सुधन्वन् पृच्छामि दुर्विवक्ता स्म किं वसेत्॥
verses.translation: Prahrada said Who does not answer, truly or falsely; I ask you, Sudhanvan, where does that wrong user of his tongue live?

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच यां रात्रिमधिविन्ना स्त्री यां चैवाक्षपराजितः। यां च भाराभितप्ताङ्गो दुर्विवक्ता स्म तां वसेत्॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said One who makes a wrong use of his tongue lives passing his nights like a woman having her husband sleeping in the arms of a co-wife or like one who has been defeated at a game at dice or like one whose body is burning with troubles.

verses.sanskrit: नगरे प्रतिरुद्धः सन् बहिरे बुभुक्षितः। अमित्रान् भूयसः पश्येद् यः साक्ष्यमनृतं वदेत्॥
verses.translation: The man, who is giving evidence tells lies, stays staving at the outer gates, shut out from the city; and he always sees his enemies.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्च पश्चनृते हन्ति दश हन्ति गवानृते। शतमश्वानृते हन्ति सहस्रं पुरुषानृते।॥
verses.translation: A lie for the sake of an animal means the degradation from heaven of five of one's ancestors; one for the sake of a cow means the downfall of ten; one for the sake of a horse means for the downfall of a hundred; while one for the sake of a man means the casting away of a thousand.

verses.sanskrit: हन्ति जातानजातांश्च हिरण्यार्थेऽनृतं वदन्। सर्वं भूम्यनृते हन्ति मा स्म भूम्यनृतं वदेः॥
verses.translation: A lie for the sake of gold means the destruction of one's race born and unborn; and one for the sake of land means the ruin of everything. Therefore do not tell lies for the sake of land.

verses.sanskrit: प्रहाद उवाच मत्तः श्रेयानङ्गिरा वै सुधन्वा स्वद्विरोचन। मातास्य श्रेयसी मातुस्तस्मात् त्वं तेन वै जितः॥
verses.translation: Prahrada said Superior to me is Angirasa; and superior to you is Sudhanvan, O Virochana. His mother, too, is superior to (your) mother. Therefore you have been vanquished by him.

verses.sanskrit: विरोचन सुधन्वाऽयं प्राणानामीश्वरस्तव। सुधन्वन् पुनरिच्छामि त्वया दत्तं विरोचनम्॥
verses.translation: O Virochana, this Sudhanvan is now the lord of your life. O Sudhanvan, I desire it (the life) to be restored by you to Virochana.

verses.sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच यद् धर्ममवृणीथास्त्वं न कामादनृतं वदीः। पुनर्ददामि ते पुत्रं तस्मात् प्रह्लाद दुर्लभम्॥
verses.translation: Sudhanvan said you have chosen virtue and have not I spoken an untruth from temptation; therefore I give back your dear son his life, O Prahrada.

verses.sanskrit: एष प्रह्लाद पुत्रस्ते मया दत्तो विरोचनः। पादप्रक्षालनं कुर्यात् कुमार्याः संनिधौ मम॥
verses.translation: This son of your, Virochana, who is given to you by me, O Prahrada, should wash my feet before the maiden. Since you

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच तस्माद् राजेन्द्र भूम्यर्थं नानृतं वक्तुमर्हसि। मा गम: ससुतामात्यो नाशं पुत्रार्थमब्रुवन्॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Therefore, O chief among kings you should not speak an untruth for the sake of land. Do not ruin yourself with your sons and ministers by refraining from speaking the truth.

verses.sanskrit: न देवा दण्डमादाय रक्षन्ति पशुपालवत्। यं तु रक्षितुमिच्छन्ति बुद्ध्या संविभजन्ति तम्॥
verses.translation: The gods do not look after men with club in hands, like the herdsman. To those they want to protect, they give intelligence.

verses.sanskrit: यथा यथा हि पुरुषः कल्याणे कुरुते मनः। तथा तथाऽस्य सर्वार्थाः सिद्ध्यन्ते नात्र संशयः॥
verses.translation: In proportion as a man is inclined towards virtue, his wishes meet with success, there is no doubt about it.

verses.sanskrit: नैनं छन्दांसि वृजिनात् तारयन्ति मायाविनं मायया वर्तमानम्। श्छन्दांस्येन प्रजहत्यन्तकाले॥
verses.translation: The Vedas do not rescue one who is deceitful and lives by deceit from sins. Indeed, the Vedas, when the end approaches, forsake him, as the full-fledge birds forsake their nests.

verses.sanskrit: मद्यपानं कलहं पूगवैरं भार्यापत्योरन्तरं ज्ञातिभेदम्। राजद्विष्टं स्त्रीपुंसयोविवाद वान्याहुर्यश्च पन्थाः प्रदुष्टः॥
verses.translation: Drinking quarrels, enmity with many, separating husband from wife (by sowing dissensions), family quarrels, disloyalty to the king, causing quarrels between husbands and wife ought, it is said, to be avoided, as well as the sinful ways of life.

verses.sanskrit: सामुद्रिकं वणिजं चोरपूर्वं शलाकधूर्तं च चिकित्सकं च। अरिं च मित्रं च कुशीलवं च नैतान् साक्ष्ये त्वधिकुर्वीत सप्त॥
verses.translation: A palmist, a merchant who has formerly been a thief, a cunning fowler, a medical man, an enemy, a friend and one who is of bad habits these seven should not be cited as witnesses.

verses.sanskrit: मानाग्निहोत्रमुत मानमौनं मानेनाधीतमुत मानयज्ञः। एतानि चत्वार्यभयंकराणि भयं प्रयच्छन्त्ययथाकृतानि।।
verses.translation: An Agnihotra ceremony performed out of vanity, silence, study out of vanity and pride based upon haughtiness, these four though not fearful of themselves, become fearful when performed unduly.

verses.sanskrit: अगारदाही गरदः कुण्डाशी सोमविक्रयी। पर्वकारश्च सूची मित्रधुक् पारदारिकः॥ भ्रूणहा गुरुतल्पी च यश्च स्यात् पानपो द्विजः। अतितीक्ष्णश्च काकच नास्तिको वेदनिन्दकः॥ स्रुवप्रग्रहणो व्रात्य: कीनाशश्चात्मवानपि। रक्षेत्युक्तश्च यो हिंस्यात् सर्वे ब्रह्महभिः समाः॥
verses.translation: 46-An incendiary, one who imprisons other persons, a pander, a wine-seller, a manufacturer of arrows, an astrologer, one who injuries friends, one who violates other's wives, one who causes miscarriage, one who violates the wife of his elders and superiors, a twice-born who drinks wine, one who uses excessively harsh words, one who opens up old hostilities, an atheist, one who speaks ill of the Vedas, one addicted to taking bribes, one whose holy thread ceremony has not been performed even through the time has come, one who secretly poisons cattle and one who injures a person who says "protect me”-all these are on the same level with those who slay Brahmanas.

verses.sanskrit: तृणोल्कया ज्ञायते जातरूपं वृत्तेन भद्रो व्यवहारेण साधुः। शूरो भयेष्वर्थकृच्छ्रेषु धीरः कृच्छ्रेष्वापत्सु सुहृदश्चारयश्च॥
verses.translation: Gold is known by fire, one of good birth by his behaviour, an honest man by his conduct, a hero in times of panic, a patient man during the time of poverty and friends and enemies during the seasons of difficulties and dangers.

verses.sanskrit: जरा रूपं हरति हि धैर्यमाशा मृत्युः प्राणान् धर्मचर्यामसूया। क्रोधः श्रियं शीलमनार्यसेवा ह्रियं कामः सर्वमेवाभिमानः॥
verses.translation: Old age destroys beauty; patience hope; death, life envy, virtue; passion, prosperity; association with the vulgar good manners; lust, modesty; and vanity, everything.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीमङ्गलात् प्रभवति प्रागल्भ्यात् सम्प्रवर्धते। दाक्ष्यात् तु कुरुते मूलं संयमात् प्रतितिष्ठति।॥
verses.translation: Prosperity has for its source good deeds; it increases owing to activity and takes root owing to skill and continues its existence owing to self-control.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टौ गुणाः पुरुषं दीपयन्ति प्रज्ञा च कौल्यं च दमः श्रुतं च। पराक्रमश्चाबहुभाषिता च दानं यथाशक्ति कृतज्ञता च॥
verses.translation: Eight qualities glorify a man, viz., wisdom, good birth, self-restraint, learning, strength, littleness of speech, gift to the best of his power and gratitude.

verses.sanskrit: एतान् गुणांस्तात महानुभावानेको गुणः संश्रयते प्रसह्या राजा यदा सत्कुरुते मनुष्यं सर्वान् गुणानेष गुणो विभाति॥
verses.translation: But, O dear, one thing alone can make all these great qualities come together. When a king honours a man, all the qualities shed luster on him.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टौ नृपेमानि मनुष्यलोके स्वर्गस्य लोकस्य निदर्शनानि। श्चत्वारि चैषामनुयान्ति सन्तः॥
verses.translation: These eight, O king, in this world of human beings are considered as the marks of heaven. Of these, four are the attributes of the good; and the honest men follow the other four.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञो दानमध्ययनं तपश्च चत्वार्येतान्यन्ववेतानि सद्भिः। दमः सत्यमार्जवमानृशंस्यं चत्वार्येतान्यनुयान्ति सन्तः।।
verses.translation: Sacrificial ceremonies, gifts, study and devotion, these tour are followed by the good. Self-control, truth, kindness and humanity, these four are also the attributes of the good.

verses.sanskrit: इज्याध्ययनदानानि तपः सत्यं क्षमा घृणा। अलोभ इति मार्गोऽयं धर्मस्याष्टविधः स्मृतः॥
verses.translation: Sacrificial ceremonies, study, gift, devotion, truth, forgiveness, mercy and contentment, these are the eight ways to virtue, according to the Smriti.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र पूर्वचतुर्वर्गो दम्भार्थमपि सेव्यते। उत्तरश्च चतुर्वर्गो नामहात्मसु तिष्ठति॥
verses.translation: The first four of these may be followed from motives of vanity; but the last four do not exist in those that are not great.

verses.sanskrit: न सा सभा यत्र न सन्ति वृद्धा वृद्धा ये न वदन्ति धर्मम्। नासौ धर्मो यत्र न सत्यमस्ति न तत् सत्यं यच्छलेनाभ्युपेतम्॥
verses.translation: That one is not an assembly where there are no old men. Those are not old men who do not speak of virtue. That is not virtue where truth does not exist; and that is not truth where deceit pervades.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यं रूपं श्रुतं विद्या कौल्यं शीलं बलं धनम्। शौर्यं च चित्रभाष्यं च दशेमे स्वर्गयोनयः॥
verses.translation: Truth, beauty, learning, knowledge, good birth, good manners, strength, wealth, heroism and ability to talk on diverse topics, these ten have their origin in heaven.

verses.sanskrit: पापं कुर्वन् पापकीर्तिः : पापमेवाश्नुते फलम्। पुण्यं कुर्वन् पुण्यकीर्तिः पुण्यमत्यन्तमश्नुते॥
verses.translation: A man, who is notoriously sinful by doing sinful acts, gets evil fruits; and one, who is reputed to be virtuous by doing virtuous acts, gains great happiness.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् पापं न कुर्वीत पुरुषः शंसितव्रतः। पापं प्रज्ञां नाशयति क्रियमाणं पुनः पुनः॥
verses.translation: There should a man firmly resolve not to do sinful acts. The sinful acts being committed again and again destroy wisdom.

verses.sanskrit: नष्टप्रज्ञः पापमेव नित्यमारभते नरः। पुण्यं प्रज्ञां वर्धयति क्रियमाणं पुनः पुनः॥
verses.translation: The man who has lost his wisdom constantly commits sin. The virtuous acts being done again and again increase wisdom.

verses.sanskrit: वृद्धप्रज्ञः पुण्यमेव नित्यमारभते नरः। पुण्यं कुर्वन् पुण्यकीर्तिः पुण्यं स्थानं स्म गच्छति। तस्मात् पुण्यं निषेवेत पुरुषः सुसमाहितः॥
verses.translation: An old man and wise always does virtuous acts. By doing virtuous acts he gains a good reputation and goes to a holy place (hereafter). Therefore should a man intently practice virtue.

verses.sanskrit: असूयको दन्दशूको निष्ठुरो वैरकृच्छठः। स कृच्छ्रे महदाप्नोति न चिरात् पापमाचरन्॥
verses.translation: A man that is envious, one that gravely injures another, one who is cruel, one who is always making enemies of others and one who is deceitful by committing sins, soon meets with grave difficulties.

verses.sanskrit: अनसूयुः कृतप्रज्ञः शोभनान्याचरन् सदा। नकृच्छ्रे महदाप्नोति सर्वत्र च विरोचते॥
verses.translation: He that is not envious and he who is wise by always doing graceful acts, never meets with any grave difficulties; and shines with luster everywhere.

verses.sanskrit: प्रज्ञामेवागमयति यः प्राज्ञेभ्यः स पण्डितः। प्राज्ञो ह्यवाप्य धर्मार्थों शक्नोति सुखमेधितुम्॥
verses.translation: He that assimilates the wisdom of the wise is himself wise; and he who is wise by doing acts, both virtuous and conducive to worldly benefits, succeeds in gaining happiness.

verses.sanskrit: दिवसेनैव तत् कुर्याद् येन रात्रो सुखं वसेत्। अष्टमासेन तत् कुर्याद् येन वर्षाः सुखं वसेत्॥
verses.translation: That act should be done by one during the day, which will enable him to live in comfort during the night; and that should be done in eight months which will enable one to live in comfort throughout the year.

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वे वयसि तत् कुर्याद् येन वृद्धः सुखं वसेत्। यावज्जीवेन तत् कुर्याद् येन प्रेत्य सुखं वसेत्॥
verses.translation: That act should be done during the early years of life, which will enable one to live in comfort during the old age. That act should be done in this life which will enable one to live in happiness after death.

verses.sanskrit: जीर्णमन्नं प्रशंसन्ति भार्यां च गतयौवनाम्। शूरं विजितसंग्रामं गतपारं तपस्विनम्॥
verses.translation: People speak well of that food which has been digested. They speak well of the wife whose youth has passed away and of that hero who has come off victorious in the battle and of that ascetic who has gone over to the other side (of life).

verses.sanskrit: धनेनाधर्मलब्धेन यच्छिद्रमपिधीयते। असंवृतं तद् भवथि ततोऽन्यदवदीर्यते॥
verses.translation: The hole, that one seeks to stop by wealth acquired by foul means, remains uncovered; and others come into existence in other places.
