verses.sanskrit: गुरुरात्मवतां शास्ता शास्ता राजा दुरात्मनाम्। अथ प्रच्छन्नपापानां शास्ता वैवस्वतो यमः॥
verses.translation: The preceptor is the controller of those who have their souls under restraint; the king is the controller of those who have bad souls; and Yama, the son of Vivasvata, is the controller of those who sin in secret.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषीणां च नदीनां च कुलानां च महात्मनाम्। प्रभवो नाधिगन्तव्यः स्त्रीणां दुश्चरितस्य च।॥
verses.translation: The greatness of Rishis, of rivers, of the banks of rivers and of the noble-minded cannot be conceived, as also the wickedness of a woman.

verses.sanskrit: द्विजातिपूजाभिरतो दाता ज्ञातिषु चार्जवी। क्षत्रियः शीलभाग् राजंश्चिरं पालयते महीम्॥
verses.translation: One attached to the worship of the twiceborn, one that makes gifts, one who behaves generously towards his cousins and the Kshatriya of good manners, rules the earth forever.

verses.sanskrit: सुवर्णपुष्पां पृथिवीं चिन्वन्ति पुरुषास्त्रयः। शूरश्च कृतविद्यश्च यश्च जानाति सेवितुम्॥
verses.translation: These three, viz., the brave, the wise and these who know how to protect others, plucks flowers of gold from the earth.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धिश्रेष्ठानि कर्माणि बाहुमध्यानि भारत। तानि जवाजघन्यानि भारप्रत्यवराणि च॥
verses.translation: Acts performed by means of the intellect are the best; those performed by the arms come next; O Bharata, those by the things are bad; while those performed by carrying loads are the worst.

verses.sanskrit: दुर्योधनेऽथ शकुनौ मूढे दुःशासने तथा। कर्णे चैश्वर्यमाधाय कथं त्वं भूतिमिच्छसि॥
verses.translation: Having entrusted your kingdom Duryodhana, Shakuni and the fool Dushasana and Karna, how can you hope for prosperity?

verses.sanskrit: सर्वैर्गुणैरुपेतास्तु पाण्डवा भरतर्षभ। पितृवत् त्वयि वर्तन्ते तेषु वर्तस्व पुत्रवत्॥
verses.translation: The Pandavas who are possessed of every virtue, O best among the race of Bharata, depend on you as their father. Do you also depends upon them as your sons. to

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच अत्रैवोदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम्। आत्रेयस्य च संवाद साध्यानां चेति नः श्रुतम्॥
verses.translation: Vidura said In this connection is quoted the old story of the conversation between the son of Atri and Saddhyas as heard by us.

verses.sanskrit: चरन्तं हसरूपेण महर्षिं संशितव्रतम्। साध्या देवा महाप्राज्ञं पर्यपृच्छन्त वै पुरा॥
verses.translation: While the great Rishi of rigid vows was wandering as a mendicant, the deities called Saddhyas, in days of old, asked him of great wisdom.

verses.sanskrit: साध्या ऊचुः साध्या देवा वयमेते महर्षे दृष्ट्वा भवन्तं न शक्नुमोऽनुमातुम्। श्रुतेन धीरो बुद्धिमांस्त्वं मतो नः काव्यां वाचं वक्तुमर्हस्युदाराम्॥
verses.translation: The Saddhyas said We are deities called Saddhyas. O great Rishi, seeing you, we are unable to guess who you are; but it seems to us that you are possessed of self-control and thorough acquaintance with the holy books. It is, therefore, most proper that you should discourse to us in the magnanimous words full of wisdom.

verses.sanskrit: हंस उवाच एतत् कार्यममराः संश्रुतं मे धृतिः शमः सत्यधर्मानुवृत्तिः। ग्रन्धि विनीय हृदयस्य सर्वं प्रियाप्रिये चात्मसमं नयीत॥
verses.translation: The mendicant Rishi said O immortals, it has been heard by me that tranquility, self-control and the observance of true religion practiced until all the knots of one's heart be loosened bring for the agreeable and disagreeable to the level of one's own self.

verses.sanskrit: आक्रुश्यमानो नाक्रोशेन्मन्युरेव तितिक्षतः आक्रोष्टारं निर्दहति सुकृतं चास्य विन्दति॥
verses.translation: The man who is reviling should not be reviled; for, the pain that is felt by him who endures (the reviling) patiently consumes the reviler and draws away and assimilates the effect of his good deeds.

verses.sanskrit: नाक्रोशी स्यान्नावमानी परस्य मित्रद्रोही नोत नीचोपसेवी। न चाभिमानी न च हीनवृत्तो रुक्षां वाचं रुषती वर्जयीत॥
verses.translation: Do not revile others, nor insult them. Do not quarrel with friends, nor associate with the vulgar. Do not be vain, nor of bad manners; and avoid words that are harsh and those that proceed from passion.

verses.sanskrit: मर्माण्यस्थीनि हृदयं तथासून् रूक्षा वाचो निर्दहन्तीह पुंसाम्। तस्माद् वाचमुषती रूक्षरूपां धर्मारामो नित्यशो वर्जयीत॥
verses.translation: Harsh words burn the very vitals, bones, heart and the life of men. Therefore he that has virtue for his refuse should always avoid harsh and angry words.

verses.sanskrit: अरुन्तुदं परुष रूक्षवाचं वाक्कण्टकैर्वितुदन्तं मनुष्यान्। विद्यादलक्ष्मीकतमं जनानां मुखे निबद्धां निर्ऋतिं वै वहन्तम्॥
verses.translation: The luckless man who pierce the hearts of others by thorns of harsh words touching their vitals bears on his face the misery and death of all men.

verses.sanskrit: भृशं सुतीक्ष्णैरनलार्कदीप्तैः। स विध्यमानोऽप्यतिदह्यमानो विद्यात् कविः सुकृतं मे दधाति॥
verses.translation: A wise men pierced by sharp arrows of words from another and blazing like the fire or the sun should, though wounded and burning with extreme pain, bear all with patience, remembering that the effects of the slanderer's good deeds become his own.

verses.sanskrit: यदि सन्तं सेवति सद्यसन्तं तपस्विनं यदि वा स्तेनमेवा वासो यथा रङ्गवशं प्रयाति तथा स तेषां वशमभ्युपैति॥
verses.translation: According as a man serves a saint or as he is wicked or virtuous or a thief, so he becomes endued with the habits of his associates; even as a cloth comes to be of the same colour with the die in which it is soaked.

verses.sanskrit: अतिवादं न प्रवदेन वादयेद् योऽनाहतः प्रतिहन्यान्न घातयेत्। हन्तुं च यो नेच्छति पापकं वै तस्मै देवाः स्पृहयन्त्यागताय॥
verses.translation: The gods court the company of one who when reviled does not return nori induces others to return and who when struck does not strike in return or make others strike and who does not wish to injure his assailant.

verses.sanskrit: अव्याहृतं व्याहृताच्छ्रेय आहुः सत्यं वदेद् व्याहृतं तद् द्वितीयम्। प्रियं वदेद् व्याहृतं तत् तृतीयं धर्म वदेद् व्याहृतं तच्चतुर्थम्॥
verses.translation: Not to speak at all is better than speaking. Secondly, if you have to speak, tell the truth. Thirdly, if you have to speak the truth, speak what is agreeable; and fourthly, if you have to speak what is agreeable, speak what is conducive to morality.

verses.sanskrit: यादृशैः संनिविंशते यादृशांश्चोपसेवते। यादृगिच्छेच्च भवितुं तादृग् भवति पूरुषः॥
verses.translation: A man becomes like him with whom he associates or him whom he serves or him like whom he seeks to be.

verses.sanskrit: यतो यतो निवर्तते ततस्ततो विमुच्यते। निवर्तनाद्धि सर्वतो न वेत्ति दुःखमण्वपि॥
verses.translation: Those things one keeps himself away from; then he is freed from everything; and the slightest misery vanishes away.

verses.sanskrit: न जीयते चानुजिगीषतेऽन्यान् न वैरकृच्चाप्रतिघातकश्चा निन्दाप्रशंसासु समस्वभावो न शोचते हृष्यति नैव चायम्॥
verses.translation: (Such a man) does vanquish others nor is vanquished by them; he never appears as other's enemy, nor their assailant; his mind remains calm at praise or blame; and he is unmoved by praise or blame.

verses.sanskrit: भावमिच्छति सर्वस्य नाभावे कुरुते मनः। सत्यवादी मृदुर्दान्तो य: स उत्तमपूरुषः॥
verses.translation: He desires prosperity for all and does not set his heart on their adversity. He is truthful, mind and can keep himself under control. And also he is the best of men.

verses.sanskrit: नानर्थकं सान्त्वयति प्रतिज्ञाय ददाति च। रन्ध्र परस्य जानाति यः स मध्यमपूरुषः॥
verses.translation: He who does not seek to solace another by telling untruth and who having promised performs and who knows the weakness of others, is a man of the middling type.

verses.sanskrit: दुःशासनस्तूपहतोऽभिशस्तो नावर्तते मन्युवशात् कृतघ्नः। न कस्यचिन्मित्रमथो दुरात्मा कलाश्चैता अधमस्येह पुंसः॥
verses.translation: Hard to get under control, unable to wear a soft look, owing to rage, when wounded by arms, ungrateful and incapable of being anybody's friend-these are the signs of an inferior man is this world.

verses.sanskrit: न श्रद्दधाति कल्याणं परेभ्योऽप्यात्मशङ्कितः। निराकरोति मित्राणि यो वै सोऽधमपूरुषः॥।
verses.translation: He, who does not appreciates benefits coming from others and who drives away all his friends, is an inferior man.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तमानेव सेवेत प्राप्तकाले तु मध्यमान्। अधमांस्तु न सेवेत य इच्छेद् भूतिमात्मनः॥
verses.translation: He who desires prosperity for himself should serve good men and, on suitable occasions, men of the middling type; but he should never serve people of the inferior type.

verses.sanskrit: प्राप्नोति वै चित्तमसद्वलेन नित्योत्थानात् प्रज्ञया पौरुषेण। न त्वेव सम्यग् लभते प्रशंसा न वृत्तमाप्नोति महाकुलानाम्॥
verses.translation: An unrighteous man obtains wealth by force, by incessant efforts, by intelligence and by prowess; but he does not win fame, properly so called, nor the wealth (virtues) of those born in high families.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच महाकुलेभ्यः स्पृहयन्ति देवा धर्मार्थनित्याश्च बहुश्रुताश्चा पृच्छामि त्वां विदुर प्रश्नमेतं भवन्ति वै कानि महाकुलानि॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said The gods prefer those born of high families, and so also those who equally recognise virtue and worldly good and those that are deeply learned. I ask you, O Vidura, this question, "Who are those born in high families?"

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच तपो दमो ब्रह्मवित्तं वितानाः पुण्या विवाहाः सततान्नदानम्। येष्वेवैते सप्त गुणा वसन्ति सम्यग्वृत्तास्तानि महाकुलानि॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Devotion, self-control, knowledge in the Vedas, sacrificial ceremonies, marriages in proper form and incessant gift of food, the families, in which these seven practices exist in proper forms, are consider to be high.

verses.sanskrit: श्चित्तप्रसादेन चरन्ति धर्मम्। ते कीर्तिमिच्छन्ति कुले विशिष्टां त्यक्तानृतास्तानि महाकुलानि॥
verses.translation: Those, who do not deviate from the right path, whose forefathers are never pained (at their wrong doings), who practice virtue with cheerfulness of heart, who desire the increase of pure fame of their families, who avoid untruths, come from high families.

verses.sanskrit: अनिज्यया कुविवाहैर्वेदस्योत्सादनेन च। कुलान्यकुलतां यान्ति धर्मस्यातिक्रमेण च॥
verses.translation: By the non-performance of sacrifices and by the performance of impure marriages, by the leaving off of the study of the Vedas, high families become degenerated, as also by insults to the Brahmanas.

verses.sanskrit: देवद्रव्यविनाशेन ब्रह्मस्वहरणेन च। कुलान्यकुलतां यान्ति ब्राह्मणातिक्रमेण च॥
verses.translation: By destruction of the wealth of the celestials, stealing the wealth of Brahmana and by the transgression of conduct of them, persons belonging to the high family comes into a low category.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणानां परिभवात् परिवादाच्च भारत। कुलान्यकुलतां यान्ति न्यासापहरणेन च॥
verses.translation: By speaking ill of the Brahmanas and by insulting them, O Bharata, the high families as become degenerated, also by the misappropriation of what has been entrusted to them..

verses.sanskrit: कुलानि समुपेतानि गोभिः पुरुषतोऽर्थतः। कुलसंख्यां न गच्छन्ति यानि हीनानि वृत्ततः॥
verses.translation: Families, even possessing cows, members and wealth, are not reckoned among families, who are of bad manners.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्ततस्त्वविहीनानि कुलान्यल्पधनान्यपि। कुलसंख्यां च गच्छन्ति कर्षन्ति च महद् यशः॥
verses.translation: Families, that are not of bad manners, though possessing but little wealth, are reckoned among families; and they win great fame.

verses.sanskrit: वृत्तं यत्नेन संरक्षेद् वित्तमेति च याति च। अक्षीणो वित्ततः क्षीणो वृत्ततस्तु हतो हतः॥
verses.translation: Good manners should be preserved with care for wealth comes and it goes. Those who are week in wealth, are not poor; but those who are weak in good manners are considered to be SO.

verses.sanskrit: गोभिः पशुभिरश्चैश्च कृष्या च सुसमृद्धया। कुलानि न प्ररोहन्ति यानि हीनानि वृत्ततः॥
verses.translation: Families well possessed of knowledge, horses and other animals and agricultural produce are not worth regarding, if they are wanting in good manners.

verses.sanskrit: मा नः कुले वैरकृत् कश्चिदस्तु राजाऽमात्यो मा परस्वापहारी। मित्रद्रोही नैकृतिकोऽनृती वा पूर्वाशी वा पितृदेवातिथिभ्यः॥
verses.translation: Let none in our family be a creator of enemies; let none be a minister to the king; none, a thief stealing other's property; none, an enemy of his well-wisher; none, deceitful; none be addicted to falsehood; and none eat before making offerings to his ancestors, gods or guests.

verses.sanskrit: यश्च नो ब्राह्मणान् हन्याद् यश्च नो ब्राह्मणान् द्विषेत्। न नः स समिति गच्छेद् यश्च नो निर्वपेत् कृषिम्।।३३।
verses.translation: None in our family who kills the Brahmanas, none in our family who injures the Brahmanas and none in our family who impedes agriculture, should associate with us.

verses.sanskrit: तृणानि भूमिरुदकं वाक् चतुर्थी च सूनृता। सतामेतानि गेहेषु नोच्छिद्यन्ते कदाचन॥
verses.translation: A straw seat, room to sit in, water and sweet words, these are never wanting in the house of the good.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धया परया राजन्नुपनीतानि सत्कृतिम्। प्रवृत्तानि महाप्राज्ञ धर्मिणां पुण्यकर्मिणाम्॥
verses.translation: These things, O king, the wise and virtuous men attached to the performance of pious acts ever keep ready for offering with reverence to their guests.

verses.sanskrit: सूक्ष्मोऽपि भारं नृपते स्यन्दनो वै शक्तो वोढुं न तथान्ये महीजाः। एवं युक्ता भारसहा भवन्ति महाकुलीना न तथान्ये मनुष्याः॥
verses.translation: As the Syandana tree, though thin, o king, can still bear weights which other large trees cannot; so persons of high families can bear the load of mental anxiety, which others cannot .

verses.sanskrit: न तन्मित्रं यस्य कोपाद् बिभेति यद् वा मित्रं शङ्कितेनोपचर्यम्। यस्मिन् मित्रे पितरीवाश्वसीत तद् वै मित्रं सङ्गत्तानीतराणि॥
verses.translation: He whose anger inspires fear or he who must be waited upon with fear is not a friend; but the friend whom one can trust as a father is a true friend. Other kinds of friendship are merely contracted in name.

verses.sanskrit: यः कश्चिदप्यसम्बद्धो मित्रभावेन वर्तते। स एव बन्धुस्तन्मित्रं सा गतिस्तत् परायणम्॥
verses.translation: That friend who, though not related in any way, yet acts as a friend; and he is a true friend, a refuge and a protector.

verses.sanskrit: चलचित्तस्य वै पुंसो वृद्धाननुपसेवतः। पारिप्लवमतेर्नित्यमध्रुवो मित्रसंग्रहः॥
verses.translation: The making of friends by that man is not certain, who is of an unsteady mind or who does not serve old men or who is not constant in his opinions or who is of a frickle disposition. men are

verses.sanskrit: चलचित्तमनात्मानमिन्द्रियाणां वशानुगम्। अर्थाः समभिवर्तन्ते हंसाः शुष्कं सरो यथा।॥
verses.translation: Prosperity forsakes those who are of unsteady minds, those who have no souls and those who are under the control of their senses, even as the swan forsakes the dried up lakes.

verses.sanskrit: अकस्मादेव कुप्यन्ति प्रसीदन्त्यनिमित्ततः। शीलमेतदसाधूनाम, पारिप्लवं यथा।॥
verses.translation: To be angry all on a sudden and to be generous without cause are the signs of unrighteous like clouds that inconstant.

verses.sanskrit: सत्कृताश्च कृतार्थाश्च मित्राणां न भवन्ति ये। तान् मृतानपि क्रव्यादाः कृतघ्नान् नोपभुञ्जते॥
verses.translation: The dead bodies of those, who, served and benefited by friends, show them ingratitude, are eaten up with disgust even by the birds of prey.

verses.sanskrit: अर्चयेदेव मित्राणि सति वाऽसति वा धने। नानर्थयन् प्रजानाति मित्राणां सारफल्गुताम्॥
verses.translation: Poor or rich, one should serve his friends. Unasked to do some service, a friend cannot make know the sincerity or insincerity of his heart.

verses.sanskrit: संतापाद् भ्रश्यते रूपं संतापाद् भ्रश्यते बलम्। संतापाद् भ्रश्यते ज्ञानं संतापाद् व्याधिमृच्छति॥
verses.translation: Sorrow destroys beauty. Sorrow destroys strength. Sorrow destroys knowledge; and sorrow brings on disease.

verses.sanskrit: अनवाप्यं च शोकेन शरीरं चोपतप्यते। अमित्राश्च प्रहृष्यन्ति मा स्म शोके मनः कृथाः॥
verses.translation: Though one's object is not gained, yet the body is consumed by grief, which makes one's enemies glad; therefore do not give way to grief.

verses.sanskrit: पुनर्नरो म्रियते जायते च पुनर्नरो हीयते वर्धते च। पुनर्नरो याचति याच्यते च पुनर्नरः शोचति शोच्यते च॥
verses.translation: A man again and again both dies and is born; a man again and again withers and grows; a man again and again asks and is asked; and a man again and again laments and is lamented for.

verses.sanskrit: सुखं च दुःखं च भवाभवौ च लाभालाभौ मरणं जीवितं च। पर्यायशः सर्वमेते स्पृशन्ति तस्माद् धीरो न च हृष्येन्न शोचेत्॥
verses.translation: Happiness and misery, prosperity and adversity, gain and loss, death and life come to all by turn; therefore he that is wise should not be glad nor sorry.

verses.sanskrit: चलानि हीमानि षडिन्द्रियाणि तेषां यद् यद् वर्धते यत्र यत्र। ततस्तत: स्रवते बुद्धिरस्य छिद्रोदकुम्भादिव नित्यमम्भः॥
verses.translation: The six senses are not constant. The understanding flows out in proportion to their strength, even as water flows out of a full pot through its holes.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच तनुरुद्धः शिखी राजा मिथ्योपचरितो मया। मन्दानां मम पुत्राणां युद्धेनान्तं करिष्यति॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said The king (Yudhishthira), who resembles the fame of fire and who has been played false by me, will put an end to the life of my wicked sons in battle.

verses.sanskrit: नित्योद्विग्नमिदं सर्वं नित्योद्विग्नमिदं मनः। यत् तत् पदमनुद्विग्नं तन्मे वद महामते॥
verses.translation: Everything seems to be a source of anxiety. Hence my mind is constantly filled with anxiety. O you of great intelligence, speak to me what is calculated to remove my anxiety.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच नान्यत्र विद्यातपसोर्नान्यत्रेन्द्रियनिग्रहात्। नान्यत्र लोभसंत्यागाच्छान्तिं पश्यामि तेऽनघ।५।।
verses.translation: Vidura said In nothing but knowledge and devotion, in nothing but the control of senses, in nothing else but the perfect abandonment of avarice, do I see your good.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्ध्या भयं प्रणुदति तपसा विन्दते महत्। गुरुशुश्रूषया ज्ञानं शान्तिं योगेन विन्दति॥
verses.translation: Knowledge removes fear and greatness is attained by devotion; and by serving one's elders and by application both knowledge and happiness are secured.

verses.sanskrit: अनाश्रिता दानपुण्यं वेदपुण्यमनाश्रिताः। रागद्वेषविनिर्मुक्ता विचरन्तीह मोक्षिणः॥
verses.translation: Those, desirous of attaining salvation without securing the merit obtainable by gifts and by the study of the Vedas, roam in this world liberated from anger and jealousy.

verses.sanskrit: स्वधीतस्य सुयुद्धस्य सुकृतस्य च कर्मणः। तपसश्च सुतप्तस्य तस्यान्ते सुखमेधते॥
verses.translation: At the end of a good course of study or at the end of a battle well fought or at the end of asceticism well performed, does happiness increase.

verses.sanskrit: स्वास्तीर्णानि शयनानि प्रपन्ना न वै भिन्ना जातु निद्रां लभन्ते। न स्त्रीषु राजन् रतिमाप्नुवन्ति न मागधैः स्तूयमाना न सूतैः॥
verses.translation: Those who are not in good terms with their blood relations get no sleep, through lying on beds well prepared; nor do they, O king, obtain pleasure from women or from the laudatory songs of professional eulogists.

verses.sanskrit: न वै भिन्ना जातु चरन्ति धर्म न वै शुकं प्राप्नुवन्तीह भिन्नाः। न वै भिन्ना गौरवं प्राप्नुवन्ति न वै भिन्नाः प्रशमं रोचयन्ति॥
verses.translation: Those who are not in good terms with their blood relations cannot practice virtue; nor can they enjoy happiness in this world; nor they can win fame; nor do they derive pleasure from peace.

verses.sanskrit: न वै तेषां स्वदते पथ्यमुक्तं योगक्षेमं कल्पते नैव तेषाम्। भिन्नानां वै मनुजेन्द्र परायणं न विद्यते किंचिदन्यद् विनाशात्॥
verses.translation: They are not pleased with what is spoken for their benefit; they cannot get hat they do not possess; nor they can retain what they have. O chief among men, there is no other end of those that are not in good terms with their blood relations save destruction.

verses.sanskrit: सम्पन्नं गोषु सम्भाव्यं सम्भाव्यं ब्राह्मणे तपः। सम्भाव्यं चापलं स्त्रीषु सम्भाव्यं ज्ञातितो भयम्॥
verses.translation: Milk is possible in cows, devotion is possible in the Brahmanas; unsteadiness is possible among women; and cause of fear may be expected from blood relations.

verses.sanskrit: तन्तवोऽs: प्यायिता नित्यं तनवो बहुलाः समाः। बहून् बहुत्वादायासान् सहन्तीत्युपमासताम्॥
verses.translation: Several thin threads of the same length collected together can bear the weight of the shuttle-cock constantly passing over them easily owing to their numerical strength.

verses.sanskrit: धूमायन्ते व्यपेतानि ज्वलन्ति सहितानि च। धृतराष्ट्रोल्मकानीव ज्ञातयो भरतर्षभ॥
verses.translation: Separated pieces of burning wood produce only smoke; but united they blaze. The same is the case, O Dhritarashtra, with blood relations.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणेषु च ये शूराः स्त्रीषु ज्ञातिषु गोषु च। वृन्तादिव फलं पक्वं धृतराष्ट्र पतन्ति ते॥
verses.translation: Those who harsh towards the Brahmanas, women, blood relations and cows fall, O Dhritarashtra, like ripe fruits from their stalks.

verses.sanskrit: महानप्येकजो वृक्षो बलवान् सुप्रतिष्ठितः। प्रसह्य एव वातेन सस्कन्धो मर्दितु क्षणात्॥
verses.translation: A large tree standing by itself, through strong and firm, can in a moment be brought down with its truck by a strong wind.

verses.sanskrit: अथ ये सहिता वृक्षाः सघशः सुप्रतिष्ठिताः। ते हि शीघ्रतमान् वातान् सहन्तेऽन्योन्यसंश्रयात्॥
verses.translation: But those trees that grow close together firmly can bear the force of stronger winds owing to their mutual support.

verses.sanskrit: एवं मनुष्यमप्येकं गुणैरपि समन्वितम्। शक्यं द्विषन्तो मन्यन्ते वायुर्दुममिवैकजम्॥
verses.translation: In the same way people consider a man, who is alone though endowed with many virtues, capable of being vanquished, like a tree standing alone by the wind. are

verses.sanskrit: अन्योन्यसमुपष्टम्भादन्योन्यापाश्रयेण च। ज्ञातयः सम्प्रवर्धन्ते सरसीवोत्पलान्युत।॥
verses.translation: Owing to mutual assistance and mutual support, blood relations grow like lotus stalks in a lake.

verses.sanskrit: अवध्या ब्राह्मणा गावो ज्ञातयः शिशवः स्त्रियः। येषां चान्नानि भुञ्जीत ये च स्युः शरणागताः॥
verses.translation: The Brahmanas, cows, blood relations, infants and women must not be killed, as also those whose food we have eaten and who have come under our protection.

verses.sanskrit: न मनुष्ये गुणः कश्चिद् राजन् सधनतामृते। अनातुरत्वाद् भद्रं ते मृतकल्पा हि रोगिणः॥
verses.translation: In a man no quality develop, O king, without wealth; but you can gain your object owing to immunity from disease. Those that are suffering from disease are like the dead.

verses.sanskrit: अव्याधिजं कटुकं शीर्षरोगि पापानुबन्धं परुषं तीक्ष्णमुष्णम्। सतां पेयं यन्न पिबन्त्यसन्तो मन्यु महाराज पिब प्रशाम्य॥
verses.translation: Anger is a drink which the unrighteous cannot swallow. It brings on pain in the end, which is bitter, pungent and hot. It ought to be swallowed up by the good. You, great king, swallow it and be pacified.

verses.sanskrit: रोगार्दिता न फलान्याद्रियन्ते न वै लभन्ते विषयेषु तत्त्वम्। दुःखोपेता रोगिणो नित्यमेव न बुध्यन्ते धनभोगान् न सौख्यम्॥
verses.translation: They that are affected with disease do not appreciate enjoyments; not do they gain any pleasure from wealth. Those that are affected with disease and so filled with sorrow do not know what enjoyment, proceeding from wealth, is.

verses.sanskrit: पुरा ह्युक्तं नाकरोस्त्वं वचो मे द्यूते जितां द्रौपदी प्रेक्ष्य राजन्। दुर्योधनं वारयेत्यक्षवत्यां कितवत्वं पण्डिता वर्जयन्ति॥
verses.translation: I told you before, O king, when I saw Draupadi won at dice-'Stop, Duryodhana; for they that are wise avoid excess at play.' You did not act accordingly.

verses.sanskrit: न तद् बलं यन्मृदुना विरुध्यते सूक्ष्मो धर्मस्तरसा सेवितव्यः। {दुप्रौढा गच्छति पुत्रपौत्रान्॥
verses.translation: That is not strength which is opposed to softness. That policy should be pursued which is fraught with virtue. The policy having crookedness at its basis is soon destroyed; but the prosperity derived from a policy, at once strong and soft, descend to one's sons and grandsons.

verses.sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्राः पाण्डवान् पालयन्तु पाण्डोः सुतास्तव पुत्रांश्च पान्तु। एकारिमित्राः कुरवो ह्येककार्या जीवन्तु राजन् सुखिनः समृद्धाः॥
verses.translation: Let the sons of Dhritarashtra, therefore, make friends of the sons of Pandu; and let the sons of Pandu make friends with your sons; let the Kurus and Pandus live having the same friends and foes, o king, being happy and prosperous.

verses.sanskrit: मेढीभूतः कौरवाणां त्वमद्य त्वय्याधीनं कुरुकुलमाजमीढ। पार्थान् बालान् वनबासप्रतप्तान् गोपायस्व स्वं यशस्तात रक्षन्॥
verses.translation: You are now the refuge of the sons of Kuru. The race of Kuru, O Ajmida, is dependent on you. O dear, preserve your fame and protect the sons of Pritha who are mere boys and who are affected with the troubles of exile.

verses.sanskrit: र्मा तेऽन्तरं रिपव: प्रार्थयन्तु। सत्ये स्थितास्ते नरदेव सर्वे दुर्योधनं स्थापय त्वं नरेन्द्र॥
verses.translation: O descendant of Kuru, make peace with the sons of Pandu; let not your enemies pry into your internal relations; they are all attached to truth. O god among men, O king among men, make Duryodhana renounce his ways.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच सप्तदशेमान् राजेन्द्र मनुः स्वायम्भुवोऽब्रवीत्। वैचित्रवीर्य पुरुषानाकाशं मुष्टिभिनतः॥ दानवेन्द्रस्य च धनुरनाम्यं नमतोऽब्रवीत्। अथो मरीचिनः पादानग्राह्यान् गृह्णतस्तथा॥
verses.translation: Vidura said O chief among kings, Manu, the descendant of the self-create. Being, has spoken of the following seventeen kinds of men as those who strike the air with fists, O son of Vichitravirya; (or as) those who seek to bend the bow of Indra, composed of vapour and to touch the rays of the sun, which cannot be touched.

verses.sanskrit: यश्चाशिष्यं शास्ति वै यश्च तुष्येद् यश्चातिबेलं भजते द्विषन्तम्। स्त्रियश्च यो रक्षति भद्रमश्नुते यशायाच्यं याचते कत्थते वा ॥ यश्चाभिजातः प्रकरोत्यकार्य यश्चाबलो बलिना नित्यवैरी। अश्रद्दधानाय च यो ब्रवीति यश्चाकाम्यं कामयते नरेन्द्र॥ बध्वावहासं श्वशुरो मन्यते यो वध्वा वसन्नभयो मानकामः। परक्षेत्रे निर्वपति यश्चबीजं स्त्रियः च यः परिवदतेऽतिवेलम्॥ यश्चापि लब्ध्वा न स्मरामीति वादी दत्त्वा च यः कत्थि याच्यमानः। यश्चासतः सत्त्वमुपानयीत एतान् नयन्ति निरयं पाशहस्ताः॥
verses.translation: (The seventeen kinds of men are), he who seeks to control one incapable of being brought under control; he who is satisfied with trifling gains; he who serves his enemies; he who controls women; he who asks favours that ought not to be asked; he who boasts, having done very little good; he who, well born, does improper acts; he who through week is always struggling with one who is powerful; he who talks to one listening with disgust; he who desires what ought not to be desired, O chief among men; he who being a father-in-law cracks jokes with his daughter-in-law; he whose fears being dispelled by his daughter-inlaw desires to be respected; he who sows his own seeds in the ground of another; and he who speaks very ill of his wife; he who having obtained a favour from another says he does nor remember it; he who having promised something makes empty boats when asked to perform it; he who seeks to prove the honesty of a dishonest person, the servitors of Yoma drag these down to hell, with noose in hand.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन् यथा वर्तते यो मनुष्यस्तस्मिंस्तथा वर्तितव्यं स धर्मः। मायाचारो मायया वर्तितव्यः साध्वाचारः साधुना, प्रत्युपेयः॥
verses.translation: It is a good policy to behave towards one in the same way he behaves towards another. One who behaves deceitfully should be served deceitfully; and one who behaves honestly should be served with honesty.

verses.sanskrit: जरा रूपं हरति हि धैर्यमाशा मृत्युः प्राणान् धर्मचर्यामसूया। कामो ह्रियं वृत्तमनार्यसेवा क्रोधः श्रियं सर्वमेवाभिमानः॥
verses.translation: Old age destroys beauty; patience, hope; death, life; the practice of virtue, worldly pleasures; desire, shame; good behaviors, companionship with the wicked; anger, prosperity; and pride, everything.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच शतायुरुक्तः पुरुषः सर्ववेदेषु वै यदा। नाप्नोत्यथ च तत् सर्वमायुः केनेह हेतुना॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said In all the Vedas, man is said to have a life of one hundred years. For what reason then do not all men attain to that age?

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच अंतिमानोऽतिवादश्च तथात्यागो नराधिप। क्रोधश्चात्मविधित्सा च मित्रद्रोहश्च तानि षट्॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Too much pride, too much of speaking, the reverse of restraint, O king and anger, quarrel with relations and enmity with friends, these six are like swords that cut off the period of life given to men. These kill men and not death. Good betide they sons (after renouncing these).

verses.sanskrit: एत एवासयस्तीक्ष्णा कृन्तन्त्यायूंषि देहिनाम्। एतानि मानवान् प्रन्ति न मृत्युर्भद्रमस्तु ते॥ विश्वस्तस्यैति यो दारान् यश्चापि गुरुतल्पगः। वृषलीपतिर्द्विजो यश्च पानपश्चैव भारत।॥ आदेशकृद् वृत्तिहन्ता द्विजानां प्रेषकश्च यः। शरणागतहा चैव सर्वे ब्रह्महणः समाः। एतैः समेत्य कर्तव्यं प्रायश्चित्तमिति श्रुतिः॥
verses.translation: He who commits adultery with those who place trust in him and who does the same with the wife of his elder, that twice-born who becomes the husband of a Shudra woman, who is given to drinking, O Bharata, who commands the twice-born or takes away their livings, who becomes their master and who kills him who demands his protection, these are like those who kill the Brahmanas. After coming in contract with one of these, expiation should be performed, which the Shrutis declare.

verses.sanskrit: गृहीतवाक्यो नयविद् वदान्यः शेषानभोक्ता ह्यविहिंसकश्च। नानर्थकृत्याकुलितः कृतज्ञः सत्यो मृदुः स्वर्गमुपैति विद्वान्॥
verses.translation: The wise man, who is skillful in speech, knows the code of morality. He eats last (i.e. after having made due offerings to the gods and Pitris). He is not envious; he does not injure others; he is skillful, grateful, truthful and humble. He attains heaven.

verses.sanskrit: सुलभाः पुरुषा राजन् सततं प्रियवादिनः। अप्रियस्य तु पथ्यस्य वक्ता श्रोता च दुर्लभः॥
verses.translation: Persons speaking agreeably can readily be met with, O king; but not so the man who speaks disagreeably.

verses.sanskrit: यो हि धर्मं समाश्रित्य भर्तुः प्रियाप्रिये। अप्रियाण्याहं पथ्यानि तेन राजा सहायवान्।॥
verses.translation: He, who having an eye on virtue and disregarding what is a agreeable or disagreeable but beneficial, is a real help to his king.

verses.sanskrit: त्यजेत् कुलार्थं पुरुषं ग्रामस्यार्थं कुलं त्यजेत्। ग्रामं जनपदस्यार्थं आत्मार्थं पृथिवीं त्यजेत्॥
verses.translation: A man should be sacrificed for the sake of a family; a family should be sacrificed for the sake of a village; a village for a kingdom; and the whole world for the soul.

verses.sanskrit: आपदर्थं धनं रक्षेद् दारान् रक्षेद् धनैरपि। आत्मानं सततं रक्षेद् दारैरपि धनैरपि॥
verses.translation: To ward off difficulties one should possess wealth; by wealth one should protect his wife; and one's own self should ever be protected by wife and wealth,

verses.sanskrit: द्यूतमेतत् पुराकल्पे दृष्टं वैरकरं नृणाम्। तस्माद् द्यूतं न सेवेत हास्यार्थमपि बुद्धिमान्॥
verses.translation: Gambling, from the early ages has been seen to be the cause of enmity among men; therefore it should not be resorted to even in jests by the wise.

verses.sanskrit: उक्तं मया द्यूतकालेऽपि राजन् नेदं युक्तं वचनं प्रातिपेया तदौषधं पथ्यमिवातुरस्य न रोचते तव वैचित्रवीर्य।।२०
verses.translation: It was said by me at the time of the play, O king, that this was not proper; but, O son of Pratipa, this speech was disagreeable to you even as medicine to a sick man, O son of Vichitravirya.

verses.sanskrit: काकैरिमांश्चित्रबर्हान् मयूरान् पराजयेथाः पाण्डवान् धार्तराष्ट्रैः। हित्वा सिंहान् क्रोष्टकान् गृहमान: प्राप्ते काले शोचिता त्वं नरेन्द्रः॥
verses.translation: By the help of these sons of Dhritarashtra who are like crows you desire to subdue the Pandavas who are as peacocks with variegated plumage. Forsaking the lions you are protecting the jackals. When the time comes, you will repent for it.

verses.sanskrit: यस्तात न क्रुध्यति सर्वकालं भृत्यस्य भक्तस्य हिते रतस्य। तस्मिन् भृत्या भर्तरि विश्वसन्ति न चैनमापत्सु परित्यजन्ति॥
verses.translation: That master, O sire, who does not often get angry with his servants, that are devoted to him and bent on furthering his in crests, commands confidence from his servants, who do not forsake him in times of danger.

verses.sanskrit: न भृत्यानां वृत्तिसंरोधनेन राज्यं धनं संजिघृक्षेदपूर्वम्। त्यजन्ति होनं वञ्चिता वै विरुद्धाः स्निग्धा ह्यमात्याः परिहीनभोगाः॥
verses.translation: Seek not to become the possessor of another's kingdom or wealth by stopping the pay of your servants. Even the affectionate ministers, defrauded and deprived of their enjoyments, turn against their master and leave him.

verses.sanskrit: ण्यायव्यये चानुरूपा च वृत्तिम्। संगृह्णीयादनुरूपान् सहायान् सहायसाध्यानि हि दुष्कराणि॥
verses.translation: Having first reflected on what things are to be done and fixing allowances to spit income and expenditure, one should make suitable alliances, Alliances accomplish what is most difficult.

verses.sanskrit: अभिप्रायं यो विदित्वा तु भर्तुः सर्वाणि कार्याणि करोत्यतन्द्री। वक्ता हितानामनुक्त आर्य: शक्तिज्ञः आत्मेव हि सोऽनुकम्प्यः॥
verses.translation: He, who after knowing the intentions of his lord, does all acts with promptitude and who though honorable and devoted to his master says what is conducive to his interests and knows his own strength, is to be regarded as his (the lord's) own self.

verses.sanskrit: वाक्यं तु यो नाद्रियतेऽनुशिष्टः प्रत्याह यश्चापि नियुज्यमानः। प्रज्ञाभिमानी प्रतिकूलवादी त्याज्यः स तादृक् त्वरयैक भृत्यः॥
verses.translation: The servant, who ordered pays no attention to the order and who enjoined uses arguments against the order through pride of wisdom and who speaks ill of his master, should be got rid of quickly.

verses.sanskrit: अस्तब्धमक्लीबमदीर्घसूत्रं सानुक्रोशं श्लक्ष्णमहार्यमन्यैः। अरोगजातीयमुदारवाक्यं दूतं वदन्त्यष्टगुणोपपन्नम्॥
verses.translation: Wanting in pride, able, quick in doing things, kind, strong, incorruptible, free from disease and pleasant of speech-one with these eight qualities should, it is said, be sent as a messenger.

verses.sanskrit: न विश्वासाज्जातु परस्य गेहे गच्छेन्नरशेतयानो विकाले। न चत्वरे निशि तिष्ठेन्निगूढो न राजकाम्यां योषितं प्रार्थयीत॥
verses.translation: A man should not, out of confidence, go to the house of an untrustworthy person in the evening. He should not hide himself in the yard of another's house at night, nor desire a woman sued by a king.

verses.sanskrit: न निछवं मन्त्रगतस्य गच्छेत् संसृष्टमन्त्रस्य कुसङ्गतस्य। न च ब्रूयान्नाश्वसिमि त्वयीति सकारणं व्यपदेशं तु कुर्यात्॥
verses.translation: One should not go against the opinions of him who keeps low company and who seeks counsel from all he comes in contract with; nor should, one say “I do not believe you;" but he should dismiss him on some presence.

verses.sanskrit: घृणी राजा पुंश्चली राजभृत्यः पुत्रो भ्राता विधवा बालपुत्रा। सेनाजीवी चोद्धृतभूतिरेव व्यवहारेषु वर्जनीयाः स्युरेते॥
verses.translation: A king who has too much of the sense of delicacy, a woman of loose character, the servant of a king, a son, a brother, a widow with a child, one who is employed in the army to get a living and one who has suffered loss of wealth-these should avoid transactions of lending and borrowing.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टौ गुणाः पुरुषं दीपयन्ति प्रज्ञा च कौल्यं च श्रुतं दमश्च। पराक्रमश्चाबहुभाषिता च दानं यथाशक्ति कृतज्ञता च॥
verses.translation: Eight qualities make a man shine viz., wisdom, high birth, learning self-control, prowess, littleness of speech, charity to the best of his power and gratitude.

verses.sanskrit: नेको गुण: संश्रयते प्रसह्या राजा यदा सत्कुरुते मनुष्यं सर्वान् गुणानेष गुणो बिभर्ति॥
verses.translation: These eight great qualities, O sire, have their source in one only when a king favours him; that incident brings on and keeps together all these qualities.

verses.sanskrit: गुणा दश स्नानशीलं भजन्ते बलं रूपं स्वरवर्णप्रशुद्धिः। स्पर्शश्च गन्धश्च विशुद्धता च श्री: सौकुमार्य प्रवराश्च नार्यः॥
verses.translation: Those who bathe get these eight qualities, viz., strength, beauty, voice, ability to pronounce all the letters correctly, delicacy of touch, fineness of scent, purity, prosperity, delicacy of limbs and beautiful women.

verses.sanskrit: गुणाश्च पण्मितभुक्तं भजन्ते आरोग्यमायुश्च बलं सुखं च। अनाविलं चास्य भवत्यपत्यं न चैनमायून इति क्षिपन्ति॥
verses.translation: The following six qualities adorn him who eats moderately, viz., immunity from disease long life, strength, happiness, the possession of children who are healthy and freedom from accusation of gluttony.

verses.sanskrit: अकर्मशीलं च महाशनं च लोकद्विष्टं बहुमायं नृशंसम्। अदेशकालज्ञमनिष्टवेषमेतान् गृहे न प्रतिवासयेत॥
verses.translation: One who does improper acts, one who eats excessively, one who is hated by men, one who is very deceitful, one who is cruel, one who does not know the suitability of time and place, one who dresses indecently-these six should not be allowed a shelter in one's house.

verses.sanskrit: कदर्यमाक्रोशकमश्रुतं च वनौकसं धूर्तममान्यमानिनम्। मेतान् भृशार्तोऽपि न जातु याचेत्॥
verses.translation: A miser, one who speaks with malice, one who is not learned, one inhabiting the woods, one who is cunning, one who does not respect another that is generally respected, one who is cruel, one who has the habit of making enemy of others, one who is ungrateful-these should not be asked for favours even by a man in distress.

verses.sanskrit: संक्लिष्टकर्माणतिप्रमादं नित्यानृतं चादृढभक्तिकं च। विसृष्टरागं पटुमानिनं चाप्येतान् न सेवेत नराधमान् षट्॥
verses.translation: One who always acts against his interests, one who always makes blunders, one who always speaks falsehood, one not firm in his devotion, one wanting in affection and one who thinks himself able to perform all tasksthese six worst classes of men should never be served.

verses.sanskrit: सहायबन्धना ह्याः सहायाश्चार्थबन्धना। अन्योन्यबन्धनावेतौ विनान्योन्यं न सिद्ध्यतः॥
verses.translation: (The gaining of) an object depends on (the nature of) the objects (sought to be gained by them). These two depend on each other; and success cannot be gained in the absence of either.

verses.sanskrit: उत्पाद्य पुत्राननृणांश्च कृत्वा वृत्तिं च तेभ्योऽनुविधाय कांचित्। स्थाने कुमारी: प्रतिपाद्य सर्वा अरण्यसंस्थोऽथ मुनिळूभूषेत्॥
verses.translation: After begetting sons and making them independent by providing for them and after giving away all the unmarried daughters to suitable bridegrooms, one should dwell in the wood like a Muni.

verses.sanskrit: हितं यत् सर्वभूतानामात्मनश्च सुखावहम्। तत् कुर्यादीश्वरे ह्येतन्मूलं सर्वार्थसिद्धये॥
verses.translation: What conduces to the good of all creatures and is the cause to happiness to one's self should be done for the sake of God. This is the root of success of all purposes of man.

verses.sanskrit: वृद्धिः प्रभावस्तेजश्च सत्त्वमुत्थानमेव च। व्यवसायश्च यस्य स्यात् तस्यावृत्तिभयं कुतः॥
verses.translation: Intelligence, energy, prowess, strength, promptitude and perseverance, why should one having these fear for a living?

verses.sanskrit: पश्य दोषान् पाण्डवैविचहे त्वं यत्र व्यथेयुरपि देवाः सशक्राः। पुत्रैर्वैरं नित्यमुद्विग्नवासो यशःप्रणाशो द्विषतां च हर्षः॥
verses.translation: Look at the disasters following a quarrel with the Pandavas, which would make the gods with Shakra sad. These are enmity with those who are like your sons, a life of continual anxiety, the destruction of fame (of the family) and joy to the enemies.

verses.sanskrit: भीष्मस्य कोपस्तव चैवेन्द्रकल्प द्रोणस्य राज्ञश्च युधिष्ठिरस्य। उत्सादयेल्लोकमिमं प्रवृद्धः श्वेतो चहस्तियेगिवापतन् खे॥
verses.translation: O you equal to Indra, the wrath of Bhima and of yourself and of Drona and of king Yudhishthira will consume this world like a big comet falling on the earth obliquely.

verses.sanskrit: तव पुत्रशतं चैव कर्णः पञ्च च पाण्डवाः। पृथिवीमनुशासेयुरखिलां सागराम्बराम्॥
verses.translation: Your hundred sons and Karna and the five Pandavas can rule the whole world bounded by the seas.

verses.sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्रा वनं राजन् व्याघ्राः पाण्डुसुता मताः। मा वनं छिन्धि सव्यानं मा व्याघ्रान नीनशन् वनात्॥
verses.translation: The sons of Dhritarashtra, 0 king, constitute the forest and in my opinion the Pandavas are the tigers. Do not cut down that forest with the tigers and do not let the tigers be driven away from the forest and be killed.

verses.sanskrit: न स्याद् वनमृते व्याघ्रान् व्याघ्रा न स्युर्ऋते वनम्। वनं हि रक्ष्यते व्याघैर्व्याघ्रान् रक्षति काननम्॥
verses.translation: There cannot be a forest without tigers; and there cannot five tigers without a forest. The forest is protected by the tigers; and the tigers are protected by the forest.

verses.sanskrit: न तथेच्छन्ति कल्याणान् परेषां वेदितुं गुणान्। यथैषां ज्ञातुमिच्छन्ति नैर्गुण्यं पापचेतसः॥
verses.translation: Evil-minded persons do not seek to know the good qualities of men; so much so they desire to know their defects.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थसिद्धिं परामिच्छन् धर्ममेवादितश्चरेत्। न हि धर्मादपैत्यर्थः स्वर्गलोकादिवामृतम्॥
verses.translation: One who desires the complete accomplishment of his objects should practice virtue from the beginning the gaining of an object is impossible without virtue as the obtaining of nectar is impossible except from heaven.

verses.sanskrit: यस्यात्मा विरतः पापात् कल्याणे च निवेशितः। तेन सर्वमिदं बुद्धं प्रकृतिर्विकृतिश्च य॥
verses.translation: All this, whether natural or artificial, is known by him whose soul has been separated from evils and fixed on good things.

verses.sanskrit: यो धर्ममर्थं कामं च यथाकालं निषेवते। धर्मार्थकामसंयोगं सोऽमुत्रेह च विन्दति॥
verses.translation: He, who pursues virtue, worldly good and desire at suitable periods, gets a combination of virtue, worldly good and desire both here and elsewhere.

verses.sanskrit: संनियच्छति यो वेगमुत्थितं क्रोधहर्षयोः। स श्रियो भाजनं राजन् यश्चापत्सु न मुह्यति॥
verses.translation: He who restrains the force arising from anger and joy is, O king, the winner of prosperity; and he who does not lose his sense in calamities also attains prosperity.

verses.sanskrit: बलं पञ्चविधं नित्यं पुरुषाणां निबोध मे। यत् तु बाहुबलं नाम कनिष्ठं बलमुच्यते॥
verses.translation: Men have always five sorts of strength, listen to me, O king. What is called strength of arms is said to be the worst.

verses.sanskrit: अमात्यलाभो भद्रं ते द्वितीयं बलमुच्यते। तृतीयं धनलाभं तु बलमाहुर्मनीषिणः॥
verses.translation: The attainment of ministers, good betide you, is said to be the second sort of strength. The wise have declared that the obtainment of wealth is the third sort of strength.

verses.sanskrit: यत् त्वस्य सहजं राजन् पितृपैतामहं बलम्। अभिजातबलं नाम तच्चतुर्थं बलं स्मृतम्॥
verses.translation: What strength is acquired from one's father and grandfather, the strength of birth, the holy books declare, is the fourth sort of strength.

verses.sanskrit: येन त्वेतानि सर्वाणि संगृहीतानि भारत। यद् बलानां बलं श्रेष्ठं तत् प्रज्ञाबलमुच्यते॥
verses.translation: That by which all these are collected, O Bharata, the strength which is superior to all sorts of strength, is said to be the strength of intellect.

verses.sanskrit: महते योऽपकाराय नरस्यं प्रभवेन्नरः। तेन वैरं समासज्य दूरस्थोऽस्मीति नाश्वसेत्॥
verses.translation: After provoking the hostility of a man who is capable of doing great injury to another, one should not console himself by saying "I am at a distance."

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीधू राजसु सर्पषु स्वाध्यायप्रभुशत्रुषु। भोगेष्वायुषि विश्वासं कः प्राज्ञः कर्तुमर्हति॥
verses.translation: Women, kings, serpents, one's own lord, enemies, enjoyments and period of life, for what wise man it is proper to put any reliance on these?

verses.sanskrit: चिकित्सकाः सन्ति न चौषधानि। न होममन्त्रा न च मङ्गलानि नाथर्वणा नाप्यगदाः सुसिद्धाः॥
verses.translation: For one who is hit by the arrow of wisdom, neither the physicians nor medicines are of any effect. Again for such a person, the mantras of the Homa, the auspicious ceremonies, the hymns of the Atharva Veda and the antidotes of poison are of no use.

verses.sanskrit: सर्पश्चाग्निश्च सिंहश्च कुलपुत्रश्च भारत। नावज्ञेया मनुष्येण सर्वे ह्येतेऽतितेजसः॥
verses.translation: A serpent, the fire, a lion and a cousin, O Bharata, are not to be disregarded by a man. All of them are really possessed of great power.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निस्तेजो महल्लोके गूढस्तिष्ठति दारुषु। न चोपयुक्ते तद् दारु यावन्नोद्दीप्यते परैः॥
verses.translation: The energy of fire is great in this world. It lurks and hides itself in the wood and yet does not consume the wood, till it is put ablaze by others.

verses.sanskrit: स एव खलु दारुभ्यो यदा निर्मथ्य दीप्यते। तद् दारु च वनं चान्यन्निर्दहत्याशु तेजसा॥
verses.translation: That thing (fire) when produced by friction of different pieces of wood burns by its excessive energy those woods and the forest along with other things.

verses.sanskrit: एवमेव कुले जाताः पावकोपमतेजसः। क्षमावन्तो निराकाराः काष्टेऽग्निरिव शेरते॥
verses.translation: In the same way, those born in high families have energy like that of fire. Of a forgiving nature, they betray no symptoms of wrath and remain still like fire in the woods.

verses.sanskrit: लताधर्मा त्वं सपुत्रः शालाः पाण्डुसुता मताः। न लता वर्धते जातु महाद्रुममनाश्रिता॥
verses.translation: You, with your sons, have the property of creepers; while, in my opinion, the sons of Pandu are like the Shala trees. But creepers do not grow without the support of a large tree.

verses.sanskrit: वनं राजंस्तव पुत्रोऽऽम्बिकेय सिंहान् वने पाण्डवांस्तात विद्धि। सिंहैविहीनं हि वनं विनश्येत् सिंहा विनश्येयुर्ऋते वनेन॥
verses.translation: O king, your son is a forest, O son of Ambika, O sire, know that the Pandavas are the trees in that forest. Deserted by the lions, the forest will be destroyed; and the lions also will be destroyed without the forest.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ऊर्ध्वं प्राणा [त्क्रामन्ति यून: स्थविर आयति। प्रत्युत्थानाभिवादाभ्यां पुनस्तान् प्रतिपद्यते॥
verses.translation: Vidura said The heart of a young man soars high, when an old man comes (to his house). He gets it back after rising up and according him a suitable welcome.

verses.sanskrit: पीठं दत्त्वा साधवेऽभ्यागताय आनीयाप: परिनिर्णिज्य पादौ। सुखं पृष्ट्वा प्रतिवेद्यात्मसंस्थां ततो दद्यादन्नमवेक्ष्य धीरः॥
verses.translation: A wise man should give good guest a seat, bring water and have his feet washed off. And he should ask him about his welfare and speak of his own matters; then again, he should offer him food after consideration (as to what food is best suitable.)

verses.sanskrit: यस्योदकं मधुपर्कं च गां च न मन्त्रवित् प्रतिगृह्णाति गेहे। लोभाद् भयादथ कार्पण्यतो वा तस्यानर्थं जीवितमाहुरायः॥
verses.translation: He, whose water, honey, curds and kine, one versed in the Mantras (Brahmana) does not accept either owing to fear (on the part of the Brahmana) or to unwillingness and miserliness (on the part of the host), lives in vain; which the wise have declared.

verses.sanskrit: चिकित्सकः शल्यकर्तावकीर्णी स्तनः क्रूरो मद्यपो भ्रूणहा च। सेनाजीवी श्रुतिविक्रायक्श्च भृशं प्रियोऽप्यतिथि!दकाहः। :॥
verses.translation: A physician, a manufacturer of arrows, one who has given up the of Brahmacharya before the proper time, a thief, a crooked-minded man, a drunkard Brahmana, one who kills the child in the womb, one who lives by serving in the army and a seller of holy books are exceedingly dear as guests, through they may be unworthy of the offer of water.

verses.sanskrit: अविक्रयं लवणं पक्वमन्त्रं दधि क्षीरं मधु तैलं घृतं चा तिला मांसं फलमूलानि शाकं रक्तं वासः सर्वगन्धा गुणाश्च॥
verses.translation: Salt, cooked-food, curds, milk, honey, oil, clarified butter, sesame, flesh, fruits and roots, herbs and vegetables, red cloths, all sorts of perfumery and molasses must not be sold (by a Brahmana).

verses.sanskrit: अरोषणो यः समलोष्ठाश्मकाञ्चनः प्रहीणशोको गतसन्धिविग्रहः। निन्दाप्रशंसोपरतः प्रियाप्रिये त्यजन्नुदासीनवदेष भिक्षुकः॥
verses.translation: He who does not yield to wrath, who values equally a piece of earth with stone or gold, who is not touched by grief who is indifferent to friendship or enmity, who cares not for praise or blame and who avoids what is agreeable and disagreeable like one who has renounced the world, is a Bhikshuka.

verses.sanskrit: नीवारमूलेदशाकवृत्तिः सुसंयतात्माग्निकार्येषु चोद्यः। वने वसन्नतिथिष्वप्रमत्तो धुरन्धरः पुण्यकृदेष तापसः॥
verses.translation: The ascetic, who lives on rice growing wild and roots and vegetables, who has his soul under control, who is ever careful if his fire (that it may not be extinguished), who living in the forest is not indifferent to his guests, is considered to be holy and the foremost of his brotherhood.

verses.sanskrit: अपकृत्य बुद्धिमतो दूरस्थोऽस्मीति नाश्वसेत्। दीर्थी बुद्धिमतोबाहू याभ्यां हिंसति हिंसितः॥
verses.translation: After doing harm to an intelligent man, one should not console himself by saying that he is at a distance. The intelligent man who has been injured has long arms to return the injury.

verses.sanskrit: न विश्वसेदविश्वस्ते विश्वस्ते नातिविश्वसेत्। विश्वासाद् भयमुत्पन्नं मूलान्यपि निकृन्तति॥
verses.translation: One must not trust another who should not be trusted, nor put too much trust on one worthy of trust. The calamity born of such trust cuts down even the very roots.

verses.sanskrit: अनीपुंर्गुप्तदाश्च संविभागी प्रियंवदः। श्लक्ष्णो मधुरवाक् स्त्रीणां न चासां वशगो भवेत्॥१०
verses.translation: One should live without showing envy, protect his wife, divide everything property and suitably, be of sweet speech and be mild and sweet in his address to his wife and yet be not her slave.

verses.sanskrit: पूजनीया महाभागाः पुण्याश्च गृहदीप्तयः। स्त्रियः श्रियो गृहस्योक्तास्तस्माद् रक्ष्या विशेषतः॥
verses.translation: Worthy of worship, highly blessed, virtuous, forming the light of their homes-such are the wives, who are the visible embodiments of household felicity. It is therefore, they should be specially protected.

verses.sanskrit: पितुरन्तःपुरं दद्यान्मातुर्दद्यान्महानसम्। गोषु चात्मसम दद्यात् स्वयमेव कृषि व्रजेत्॥
verses.translation: The supervision of the inner apartments should be entrusted to one's father, (that of) the kitchen should be entrusted to his mother, (that of) the cows should be entrusted to some body considered equal to himself; but the supervision of agriculture should be attended to by himself.

verses.sanskrit: भृत्यैर्वाणिज्यचारं च पुत्रैः सेवेत च द्विजान्। अद्ध्योऽग्निर्ब्रह्मतः क्षत्रमश्मनो लोहमुस्थितम्॥
verses.translation: The guests who carry on commerce should be waited on by servants; but the quests of the twice-born caste by one's sons. Out of water has arisen fore; and out of Brahma, the Kshatriya; and out of stone, iron.

verses.sanskrit: तेषां सर्वत्रगं तेज: स्वासु योनिषु शाम्यति। नित्यं सन्तः कुले जाताः पावकोपमतेजसः॥
verses.translation: The force of these extend to everything; but it is neutralized of what it is born. Those good people who are born in high families have always a spirit similar to fire.

verses.sanskrit: क्षमावन्तो निराकाराः काष्ठेऽग्निरिव शेरते। यस्य मन्त्रं न जानन्ति बाहाचाभ्यन्तराश्च ये॥ स राजा सर्वतश्चक्षुश्चिरमैश्वर्यमश्नुते। करिष्यन् न प्रभाषेत कृतान्येव तु दर्शयेत्॥
verses.translation: Those born is high families are endued with a forgiving nature; and stay like fire in the wood without showing any outward symptoms. The king, whose intentions cannot be known by outsiders and even by his own officers and who has his eyes everywhere, enjoys long prosperity. One should not speak of what he wants to do, nor show what he has already done.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मकामार्थकार्याणि तथा मन्त्रो न भिद्यते। गिरिपृष्ठमुपारुह्य प्रासादं वा रहोगतः॥
verses.translation: Let not the intention, to do acts to be done either for virtue, worldly good or gratifying desires, be made known to others. Having ascended the top of a mountain or the roof of a palace.

verses.sanskrit: अरण्ये निःशलाके वा तत्र मन्त्रोऽभिधीयते। नासुहृत् परमं मन्त्रं भारतार्हति वेदितुम्॥
verses.translation: Or proceeding to a forest shorn of grass, one should think of his purposes. One who is not a friend, O Bharata, should not be made aware of important secrets.

verses.sanskrit: अपण्डितो वापि सुहृत् पण्डितो वाप्यनात्मवान्। नापरीक्ष्य महीपालः कुर्यात् सचिवमात्मनः॥
verses.translation: Nor a friend who is not learned, nor a learned friend who has not his mind under control. a king should not appoint any body his own counselor without a trial.

verses.sanskrit: अमात्ये ह्यर्थलिप्सा च मन्त्ररक्षणामेव च। कृतानि सर्वकार्याणि यस्य पारिषदा विदुः॥ धर्मे चार्थे च कामे च स राजा राजसत्तमः। गूढमन्त्रस्य नृपतेस्तस्य सिद्धिरसंशयम्॥
verses.translation: On the minister depend the desire for wealth (of the king) and the carrying out of his intentions. He, whose followers know all acts only after they are done or acts done for the sake of virtue, worldly good or gratification of desire, is the chief among kings, O king. The success of that king, whose intentions are not known is undoubted.

verses.sanskrit: अप्रशस्तानि कार्याणि यो मोहादनुतिष्ठति। स तेषां विपरिभ्रंशाद् भ्रंश्यते जीवितादपि।॥
verses.translation: He who does uncommendable acts out of ignorance loses even his life, owing to the unexpected consequences of his acts.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मणां तु प्रशस्तानामनुष्ठानं सुखावहम्। तेषामेवाननुष्ठानं पश्चात्तापकरं मतम्॥
verses.translation: The doing of acts that are commendable leads to happiness; the omission to do these acts causes injury afterwards, which is my opinion.

verses.sanskrit: अनधीत्य यथा वेदान् न विप्रः श्राद्धमर्हति। एवमश्रुतषाङ्गण्यो न मन्त्रं श्रोतुमर्हति॥
verses.translation: As a Brahmana, without studying the Vedas, is unfit to officiate at a Shradha, so one who has not heard of the six means (for protecting a kingdom) is not fit to hear of the purposes (of a king).

verses.sanskrit: स्थानवृद्धिक्षयज्ञस्य षाडण्यविदितात्मनः। अनवज्ञातशीलस्य स्वाधीना पृथिवी नृप॥
verses.translation: One, who knows about the increase, decrease and surplus of revenue in the kingdom, who knows the six means and himself, one whose conduct is never spoken ill of, has the world under his subjection.

verses.sanskrit: अमोधक्रोधहर्षस्य स्वयं कृत्यान्ववेक्षिणः। आत्मप्रत्ययकोशस्य वसुदैव वसुन्धरा॥
verses.translation: He whose wrath and joy are unfailing (to produce some results), who himself superiors over what is to be done, who has his treasury under his own control, has the world under his subjection.

verses.sanskrit: नाममात्रेण तुष्येत छत्रेण च महीपतिः। भृत्येभ्यो विसृजेदर्थान् नैकः सर्वहरो भवेत्॥
verses.translation: The lord of the earth should be satisfied with the name he earns and the umbrella (held over his head as a sign of royalty) and should divided the wealth among his servants and not be the sole possessor of everything.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणं ब्राह्मणो वेद भर्ता वेद स्त्रियं तथा। अमात्यं नृपतिर्वेद राजा राजानमेव च॥
verses.translation: A Brahmana knows a Brahmana in the same way as the nusband knows the wife. A king knows his minister and a monarch knows a monarch.

verses.sanskrit: न शत्रुर्वशमापन्नो मोक्तव्यो वध्यतां गतः। न्यग्भूत्वा पर्युपासीत वध्यं हन्याद् बले सति। अहताद्धि भयं तस्माज्जायते नचिरादिव॥
verses.translation: An enemy who deserves death should not be set liberty when under control. when one is weak, an enemy should be flattered though deserving of death; but he should be killed on the acquirement of strength. When not killed, dangers arise from him soon.

verses.sanskrit: दैवतेषु प्रयत्नेन राजसु ब्राह्मणेषु च। नियन्तव्यः सदा क्रोधो वृद्धबालातुरेषु च।॥
verses.translation: The resentment against the gods, the king, the Brahmanas should ever be suppressed with an effort; as also that against old men, children and helpless person.

verses.sanskrit: निरर्थं कलहं प्राज्ञो वर्जयेन्मूढसेवितम्। कीर्ति च लभते लोके न चानर्थेन युज्यते॥
verses.translation: A wise man should avoid unprofitable quarrels, to which fools alone are parties. By this one gains fame in this world and avoids misery.

verses.sanskrit: प्रसादो निष्फलो यस्य क्रोधश्चापि निरर्थकः। न तं भर्तारमिच्छन्ति षण्डं पतिमिव स्त्रियः॥
verses.translation: Him whose favour is without consequence and whose wrath does no harm the people do not desire to have for a lord; even as women do not want to have an impotent man to be their husband.

verses.sanskrit: न बुद्धिर्धनलाभाय न जाड्यसमृद्धये। लोकपर्यायवृत्तान्तं प्राज्ञो जानाति नेतरः॥
verses.translation: Intelligence is not for the acquirement of wealth; and laziness is not the cause of adversity. The wise man knows the history of the different conditions of man; but others do not.

verses.sanskrit: विद्याशीलवयोवृद्धान् बुद्धिवृद्धांश्च भारत। धनाभिजातवृद्धांश्च नित्यं मूढोऽवमन्यते॥
verses.translation: Fools have ever made light of those who are learned, old in age, old in intelligence, superior in wealth in wealth and in birth, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: अनार्यवृत्तमप्राज्ञमसूयकमधार्मिकम्। अनर्थाः क्षिप्रमायान्ति वाग्दुष्टं क्रोधनं तथा॥
verses.translation: Misery soon comes on him who leads a vicious life, who is wanting in wisdom, who is envious and sinful and who is wicked in speech and given to anger.

verses.sanskrit: अविसंवादनं दानं समयस्याव्यतिक्रमः आवर्तयन्ति भूतानि सम्यकप्रणिहिता च वाक्॥
verses.translation: Absence of deceitfulness, gift, observance of the respect due to others, subjugate all creature, so does well conducted speech.

verses.sanskrit: अविसंवादको दक्षः कृतज्ञो मतिमानृजुः। अपि संक्षीणकोशोऽपि लभते परिवारणम्॥
verses.translation: He, who has no deceit in him, who is skillful, grateful, intelligent and simple minded, gets friends even if his treasury has been drained off.

verses.sanskrit: धृतिः शमो दमः शौचं कारुण्यं वागनिष्ठुरा। मित्राणां चानभिद्रोहः सप्तैताः समिधः श्रियः॥
verses.translation: Wisdom, calmness of mind, self-control, holiness, the reverse of harshness in speech and the intention never to injure one's friends, these seven are to prosperity, even as fuel to the fire.

verses.sanskrit: असंविभागी दुष्टात्मा कृतघ्नो निरपत्रपः। तादृड्नराधिपो लोके वर्जनीयो नराधिप॥
verses.translation: Who does not give to others their dues, who is of a vicious soul and ungrateful and who has no sense of shame-such a wretch should, O king, be avoided in this world.

verses.sanskrit: न च रात्रौ सुखं शेते ससर्प इव वेश्मनि। यः कोपयति निर्दोष सदोषोऽभ्यन्तरं जनम्॥
verses.translation: He cannot sleep at night in peace, like one having a serpent in the room; who, being a guilty person himself, provokes other people against one who is innocent.

verses.sanskrit: येषु दुष्टेषु दोषः स्याद् योगक्षेमस्य भारत। सदा प्रसादनं तेषां देवतानामिवाचरेत्॥
verses.translation: Persons, who when tainted with a vice are capable to injuring others by depriving them of their possessions and removing the means of their livelihood, should ever be propitiated like the gods with promptitude.

verses.sanskrit: येऽर्थाः स्त्रीषु समायुक्ताः प्रमत्तपतितेषु च। ये चानार्ये समासक्ताः सर्वे ते संशयं गताः॥
verses.translation: Those objects which depend for their obtainment on women, men whose mind has been distracted, men who have fallen from their original state of purity and those that depend on the vicious, are all doubtful of fulfillment.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र स्त्री यत्र कितवो बालो यत्रानुशासिता। मज्जन्ति तेऽवशा राजन् नद्यामश्मप्लवा इव॥
verses.translation: Where a woman or where a child or where a wicked man is the guide, there, O king, persons (who make them guides) are as helpless as a stone in a river, which sinks.

verses.sanskrit: प्रयोजनेषु ये सक्ता न विशेषेषु भारत। तानहं पण्डितान् मन्ये विशेषा हि प्रसङ्गिनः॥
verses.translation: Those that are capable of grasping the general principle of things, through they may be ignorant of details, are learned men. This is my opinion, O Bharata; for details are small matters.

verses.sanskrit: यं प्रशंसन्ति कितवा यं प्रशंसन्ति चारणाः। यं प्रशंसन्ति बन्धक्यो न स जीवति मानवः॥
verses.translation: The man, whom deceitful persons praise, whom spies praise and whom immoral women praise, does not live (long).

verses.sanskrit: हित्वा तान् परमेष्वासान् पाण्डवानमितौजसः। आहितं भारतैश्वर्यं त्वया दुर्योधने महत्॥
verses.translation: Having abandoned those mighty bowmen, the Pandavas of unlimited prowess, you have, O Bharata, entrusted a great lordship on Duryodhana.

verses.sanskrit: तं द्रक्ष्यसि परिभ्रष्टं तस्मात् त्वमचिरादिव। ऐश्वर्यमदसम्मूढं बलिं लोकत्रयादिव॥
verses.translation: You shall soon see him fallen from that lordship, even as foolish Vali, who was proud of his power fallen from the three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच अनीश्वरोऽयं पुरुषो भवाभवे सूत्रप्रोता दारुमयीव योषा। धात्रा तु दिष्टस्य वशे कृतोऽयं तस्माद् वद त्वं श्रवणो धृतोऽहम्॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said Man is not the creator of his happiness or misery like a wooden doll moved by a thrcad. He has been made subject to the Fates by Providence. Therefore speak on; I am patiently hearing you.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच अप्राप्तकाल वचनं बृहस्पतिरपि ब्रुवन्। लभते बुद्ध्यवज्ञानमवमानं च भारत॥
verses.translation: Vidura said Even Brihaspati by speaking when suitable time has not come gets a charge of ignorance and insult, O Bharata.

verses.sanskrit: प्रियो भवति दानेन प्रियवादेन चापरः। मन्त्रमूलबलेनान्यो य: प्रियः प्रिय एव सः॥
verses.translation: By gift (a man) becomes agreeable; another by sweet speech; a third by the strength of incantations and drugs; but he that is (naturally) agreeable is (always) agreeable.

verses.sanskrit: द्वेष्यो न साधुर्भवति न मेधावी न पण्डितः। प्रिये शुभानि कार्याणि द्वेष्ये पापानि चैव ह॥
verses.translation: He that is despised is not honest, nor intelligent, nor wise (to the man who despises him). To him that is beloved are attributed all good acts and to him that is despised all bad ones.

verses.sanskrit: उक्तं मया जातमात्रेऽपि राजन् दुर्योधनं त्यज पुत्रं त्वमेकम्। रस्यात्यागात् पुत्रशतस्य नाशः॥
verses.translation: I told you, as soon as he was born. O king, abandon this one son, Duryodhana. By his abandonment (you will see) the proper development of your hundred sons; and by not abandoning him, there will be the destruction of your hundred sons.

verses.sanskrit: न वृद्धिर्बहु मन्तव्या या वृद्धिः क्षयमावहेत्। क्षयोऽपि बहु मन्तव्यो यः क्षयो वृद्धिमावहेत्॥
verses.translation: That gain should not be highly thought of, which brings on a little loss; and that loss should be considered a great one, which brings on a little gain.

verses.sanskrit: न स क्षयो महाराज यः क्षयो वृद्धिमावहेत्। क्षयः स त्विह मन्तव्यो लङ्वा बहु नाशयेत्॥
verses.translation: That, O great king, is not loss which brings on gain; but that should be regarded as loss which being gained causes much loss.

verses.sanskrit: समृद्धा गुणत: केचिद् भवन्ति धनतोऽपरे। धनवृद्धान् गुणै_नान् धृतराष्ट्र विवर्जय॥
verses.translation: Some become eminent thought their good qualities; others through their wealth. Avoid, O Dhritarashtra, those who are superior in wealth, but devoid of good qualities.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच सर्वं त्वमायतीयुक्तं भाषसे प्राज्ञसम्मतम्। न चोत्सहे सुतं त्यक्तुं यतो धर्मस्ततो जयः॥
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said All that you say is agreed on by the wise and is calculated to do good to me; but I cannot abandon my son. Victory comes where there is righteousness.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाच अतीवगुणसम्पन्नो न जातु विनयान्वितः। सुसूक्ष्ममपि भूतानामुपमर्दमुपेक्षते॥
verses.translation: Vidura said He that is exceedingly good and is endued with humility does not neglect even the slightest suffering of any creature (without an attempt at alleviating them).

verses.sanskrit: परापवादनिरताः परदुःखोदयेषु च। परस्परविरोधे च यतन्ते सततोत्थिताः॥
verses.translation: Those that are ever engaged in speaking ill of others ever engage themselves in quarrels which give pain to others with great care.

verses.sanskrit: सदोषं दर्शनं येषां संवासे सुमहद् भयम्। अर्थादाने महान् दोषः प्रदाने च महद् भयम्॥
verses.translation: Those whose very sight is vicious and association with whom is attended with great fear-there is great harm in giving them wealth and also there is great fear in accepting wealth from them.

verses.sanskrit: ये वै भेदनशीलास्तु सकामा निस्त्रपाः शठाः। ये पापा इति विख्याताः संवासे परिगर्हिताः॥
verses.translation: Those whose habit is to cause disagreements, those who covetous, shameless and vicious are well known as unrighteous. The association with them should be avoided.

verses.sanskrit: युक्ताश्चान्यैहादोषैर्ये नरास्तान् विवर्जयेत्। निवर्तमाने सौहार्दे प्रीतिर्नीचे प्रणश्यति॥
verses.translation: Those men who are endued with similar other great vices should be avoided. In the absence of (the cause of) friendship, the good feeling towards the low is destroyed.

verses.sanskrit: या चैव फलनिर्वृत्तिः सौहृदे चैव यत् सुखम्। यतते चापवादाय यत्नमारभते क्षये।॥
verses.translation: As also the results of connection and the happiness derived from it. They (the lowminded) then try to speak ill (of their late friend) and injure him. are

verses.sanskrit: अल्पेऽप्यपकृते मोहान शान्तिमधिगच्छति। तादृशैः संगतं नीचैर्नृशंसैरकृतात्मभिः॥
verses.translation: Even when slightly injured, they out of the want of self-control get no peace of mind. Companionship of one's self with such lowminded and heartless individuals should not be effected.

verses.sanskrit: निशम्य निपुणं बुद्ध्या विद्वान् दूराद् विवर्जयेत्। यो ज्ञातिमनुगृह्णाति दरिद्रं दीनमातुरम्॥ स पुत्रपशुभिवृद्धिं श्रेयश्चानन्त्यमश्नुते। ज्ञातयो वर्धनीयास्तैर्य इच्छन्त्यात्मनः शुभम्॥
verses.translation: A wise man examining carefully by his intelligence avoids from a distance (such persons). He who assists the poor and helpless cousins obtains an increase of children and animals and unending prosperity. Cousins should be assisted by those who desire their own happiness.

verses.sanskrit: कुलवृद्धिं च राजेन्द्र तस्मात् साधु समाचर। श्रेयसा योक्ष्यते राजन् कुर्वाणो ज्ञातिसक्रियाम्॥
verses.translation: Therefore do you that, О chief among kings, which will effect an increase of your family; by doing good to your relatives, O monarch, you will meet with prosperity.

verses.sanskrit: विगुणा ह्यपि संरक्ष्या ज्ञातयो भरतर्षभ। किं पुनर्गुणवन्तस्ते त्वत्प्रसादाभिकाक्षिणः॥
verses.translation: Cousins, though devoid of good qualities, should be protected, O foremost of the Bharata race. Should they not be protected, who are endued with good qualities and desirous of your favours?

verses.sanskrit: प्रसादं कुरु वीराणां पाण्डवानां विशाम्पते। दीयन्तां ग्रामकाः केचित् तेषां वृत्त्यर्थमीश्वर॥
verses.translation: Do you favour the heroic Pandavas, O Lord the universe. Give them some few villages for their living, O Lord.

verses.sanskrit: एवं लोके यशः प्राप्तं भविष्यति नराधिप। वृद्धेन हि त्वया कार्यं पुत्राणां तात शासनम्॥
verses.translation: By doing this, you will obtain fame in this world, O ruler of men. O sire, your sons should be controlled by you who are old.

verses.sanskrit: मया चापि हितं वाच्, विद्धि मां त्वद्धितैषिणम्। ज्ञातिभिर्विचहस्तात न कर्तव्यः शुभार्थिना। सुखानि सह भोज्यानि ज्ञातिभिर्भरतर्षभ।॥
verses.translation: What I speak is your good; know me to be your well-wisher; a man who desires happiness should not quarrel with the cousins. Happiness should be injured along with one's cousins, O best of the Bharata race.

verses.sanskrit: सम्भोजनं संकथनं सम्प्रीतिश्च परस्परम्। ज्ञातिभिः सह कार्याणि न विरोधः कदाचन॥
verses.translation: To eat together, to talk together and to love one another-these are what should be done among cousins and quarrels never.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञातयस्तारयन्तीह ज्ञातयो मज्जयन्ति च। सुवृत्तास्तारयन्तीह दुर्वृत्ता मज्जयन्ति च॥
verses.translation: In this world cousins come to the rescue and cousin sink (cousins); those who lead virtuous lives are rescued and those that lead vicious lives sink.

verses.sanskrit: सुवृत्तो भव राजेन्द्र पाण्डवान् प्रति मानद। अधर्षणीयः शत्रूणां तैर्वृतस्त्वं भविष्यसि॥
verses.translation: O chief among kings, you act like one leading virtuous life, towards the Pandavas. O you giver of honours, surrounded by them you will be unconquerable by your enemies.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीमन्तं ज्ञातिमासाद्य यो ज्ञातिरवसीदति। दिग्धहस्तं मृग इव स एनस्तस्य विन्दति॥
verses.translation: If having come face to face with a cousin who is prosperous, one shrinks like a deer in the presence of a man armed with arrows; then cousin gets transferred on his own shoulders the sins of his less prosperous cousin.

verses.sanskrit: पश्चादपि नरश्रेष्ठं तव तापो भविष्यति। तान् वा हतान् सुतान् वापि श्रुत्वा तदनुचिन्तय॥
verses.translation: Afterwards, O chief among men, you will have repentance at hearing of the death of the Pandavas or of your sons. Think of this.

verses.sanskrit: येन खट्वां समारूढः परितप्येत कर्मणा। आदावेव न तत् कुर्यादध्रुवे जीविते सति॥
verses.translation: That act which is sure to be repented for when a man enters the Vale of Death should never be done from the very beginning in this life.

verses.sanskrit: न कश्चिन्नापनयते पुमानन्यत्र भार्गवात्। शेषसम्प्रतिपत्तिस्तु बुद्धिमत्स्वेव तिष्ठति॥
verses.translation: It is not true that any other man except Bhargava has done a sinful act but a conception of the probable effects of an action is present in the mind of every intelligent person.

verses.sanskrit: दुर्योधनेन यद्येतत् पापं तेषु पुरा कृतम्। त्वया तत् कुलवृद्धेन प्रत्यानेनं नरेश्वर॥
verses.translation: If this wrong has been done towards them by Duryodhana in the first instance, it is your duty, being the foremost of his race, to undo it.

verses.sanskrit: तांस्त्वं पदे प्रतिष्ठाप्य लोके विगतकल्मषः। भविष्यसि नरश्रेष्ठ पूजनीयो मनीषिणाम्॥
verses.translation: You having reinstated them in their position, will be freed from all your sins in this world and, O chief among men, you will be worshipped by all intelligent men.

verses.sanskrit: सुव्याहृतानि धीराणां फलतः परिचिन्त्य यः। अध्यवस्यति कार्येषु चिरं यशसि तिष्ठति॥
verses.translation: He, who engages in acts after thinking of their effects as prophesied by wise men for the good of mankind, ever remains famous.

verses.sanskrit: असम्यगुपयुक्तं हि ज्ञानं सुकुशलैरपि। उपलभ्यं चाविदितं विदितं चाननुष्ठितम्॥
verses.translation: The lessons taught even by skillful men is not perfect; for the moral sought to be conveyed by them is not understood and if understood, is not carried into practice.

verses.sanskrit: पापोदयफलं विद्वान् यो नारभति वर्धते। यस्तु पूर्वकृतं पापमविमृश्यानुवर्तते। अगाधपङ्गे दुर्मेधा विषमे विनिपात्यते॥
verses.translation: That wise man, who dose not do acts which result in sin, grows (in prosperity); but the man of slow intelligence who continues in his sinful course commenced before,

verses.sanskrit: मन्त्रभेदस्य षट् प्राज्ञो द्वाराणीमानि लक्षयेत्। अर्थसंततिकामश्च रक्षेदेतानि नित्यशः॥
verses.translation: Falls into a deep mire which he cannot cross. A wise man should have an eye on the following six means of secrets being divulged.

verses.sanskrit: मदं स्वप्नमविज्ञानमाकारं चात्मसम्भवम्। दुष्टामात्येषु विश्रम्भं दूताच्चाकुशलादपि॥ द्वाराण्येतानि यो ज्ञात्वा संवृणोति सदा नृप। त्रिवर्गाचरणे युक्तः स शत्रूनधितिष्ठति॥ न वै श्रुतमविज्ञाय वृद्धाननुपसेव्य वा। धर्मार्थों वेदितुं शक्यौ बृहस्पतिसमैरपि।॥
verses.translation: He who desires wealth and children should ever keep himself away from these-vanity, dream, knowledge that spies have been set on, one's demeanor which depends on his thoughts, excessive trust on wicked ministers and incompetent ambassadors. He who being fully aware of these six door ( through which secrets ooze out). O king, keeps them always closed and engages in the attainment of the three kinds of objects (virtue, worldly good and desire), triumph over his enemies. Without a knowledge of the holy books and without proper reverence to old people.

verses.sanskrit: नष्टं समुद्रे पतितं नष्टं वाक्यमशृण्वति। अनात्मनि श्रुतं नष्टं नष्ट हुतमनग्निकम्॥
verses.translation: Virtue and profit cannot be known even by one equal (intelligent) to Brihaspati; A thing is lost when it falls into the ocean. So an advice is lost when it is not listened.

verses.sanskrit: मत्या परीक्ष्य मेधावी बुद्ध्या सम्पाद्य चासकृत्। श्रुत्वा दृष्ट्वाथ विज्ञाय प्राझैमैत्री समाचरेत्॥
verses.translation: The holy books are lost on those who have no souls of their own (have no control over them); and an offering of clarified butter is lost when poured on what is not fire (i.e. when the fire has been extinguished and ashes only remain). Having examined by his intelligence and searched by his understanding.

verses.sanskrit: अकीर्ति विनयो हन्ति हन्त्यनर्थं पराक्रमः। हन्ति नित्यं क्षमा क्रोधमाचारो हन्त्यलक्षणम्॥
verses.translation: And having heard the opinions of other men seen and known the life of the men, a wise man forms friendship with wise men. Humility removes bad repute and prowess puts an end to adversity.

verses.sanskrit: परिच्छदेन क्षेत्रेण वेश्मना परिचर्यया। परीक्षेत कुलं राजन् भोजनाच्छादनेन च॥
verses.translation: Forgiveness ever destroys anger; good behaviour neutralises the effects of evil omens in the appearance of a man. By means of one's dress, sphere of action, house, behaviour,

verses.sanskrit: उपस्थितस्य कामस्य प्रतिवादो न विद्यते। अपि निर्मुक्तदेहस्य कामरक्तस्य किं पुनः॥
verses.translation: Should a man's lineage be jaded about, O king and also by his food and dress. There is no struggling with a desire when it come on.

verses.sanskrit: प्राज्ञोपसेविनं वैद्यं धार्मिकं प्रियदर्शनम्। मित्रवन्तं सुवाक्यं च सुहृदं परिपालयेत्॥
verses.translation: Even in men who have attained salvation not to speak of those who are slaves to their desires. One who worships the wise is learned, virtuous and of a mild look.

verses.sanskrit: दुष्कुलीनः कुलीनो वा मर्यादां यो न लङ्घयेत्। धर्मापेक्षी मृदुह्रीमान् स कुलीनशताद् वरः॥
verses.translation: And has friends, is one, who of agreeable speech, has good heart, should me protected. He, who whether comes of a high or a low family, dose not transgress the houour due to others.

verses.sanskrit: ययोश्चित्तेन वा चित्तं निभृतं निभृतेन वा। समेति प्रज्ञया प्रज्ञा तयोमैत्री न जीर्यति॥
verses.translation: Who leans to virtue, who is graced with humility, who has a sense of modesty, is better than a hundred men coming of high families. When the heart of one agrees with that of another, then the secret pursuits of the former are the same as those of the latter.

verses.sanskrit: दुर्बुद्धिमकृतप्रज्ञं छन्नं कूपं तृणैरिवा विवर्जयीत मेधावी तस्मिन् मैत्री प्रणश्यति॥
verses.translation: And the learning of the former is the same as that of the latter, the friendship between the two person never wears out. One who has a wicked soul, one whose understanding has not been cultivated.

verses.sanskrit: अवलिप्तेषु मूर्खेषु रौद्रसाहसिकेषु च। तथैवापेतधर्मेषु न मैत्रीमाचरेद् बुधः॥
verses.translation: Should be shunned by an intelligent man like a well covered over with grass growing over it; friendship with them does not last. With those that are proud, ignorant, fierce and rash.

verses.sanskrit: कृतज्ञं धार्मिकं सत्यमक्षुद्रं दृढभक्तिकम्। जितेन्द्रियं स्थितं स्थित्यां मित्रमत्यागि चेष्यते॥
verses.translation: Wise men should never contract friendship, nor with them who have fallen from the path of virtue. The man who is grateful, virtuous, the reverse of mean, has firm devotion.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियाणामनुत्सर्गो मृत्युनापि विशिष्यते। अत्यर्थं पुनरुत्सर्गः सादयेद् दैवतान्यपि॥
verses.translation: And has his senses under control and attaches himself to what ought to be attached to should be desired for a friend. The renouncement of the objects of the senses is worse than death difficult of practices.

verses.sanskrit: मार्दवं सर्वभूतानामनसूया क्षमा धृतिः। आयुष्याणि बुधाः प्राहुर्मित्राणां चाविमानना॥
verses.translation: On the other hand, leaving them to be too much exercised would ruin the gods even; humility, kindness to all creatures, forgiveness and patience.

verses.sanskrit: अपनीतं सुनीतेन योऽर्थं प्रत्यानिनीषते। मतिमास्थाय सुदृढां तदकापुरुषव्रतम्॥
verses.translation: Conduce to life, the wise men have said; as also a constant respect for friends. He who desires again to accomplish, by virtuous means, an object that has once been frustrated,

verses.sanskrit: आयत्यां प्रतिकारशस्तदात्वे दृढनिश्चयः। अतीते कार्यशेषज्ञो नरोऽथैर्न प्रहीयते॥
verses.translation: And is in his right senses, the determination of that man is firm. The man who knows what remedy ought to be applied in future, who has a firm determination on present matters.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मणा मनसा वाचा यदभीक्ष्णं निषेवते। तदेवापहरत्येनं तस्मात् कल्याणमाचरेत्॥
verses.translation: And who knows (in beginning) how acts done in the past would end accomplishes all his objects. That which a man follows in deed, thought or speech.

verses.sanskrit: मङ्गलालम्भनं योगः श्रुतमुत्थानमार्जवम्। भूतिमेतानि कुर्वन्ति सतां चाभीक्ष्णदर्शनम्॥
verses.translation: Draws him away to itself; therefore a virtuous policy should be followed. Efforts for the attainment of one's holy objects, knowledge of the holy books, simplemindedness.

verses.sanskrit: अनिर्वेदः श्रियो मूलं लाभस्य च शुभस्य च। महान् भवत्यनिर्विण्णः सुखं चानन्त्यमश्नुते॥
verses.translation: These bring on prosperity; so do constant meetings with the good. Perseverance is the root the of prosperity and of gain and of happiness.

verses.sanskrit: नातः श्रीमत्तरं किंचिदन्यत् पथ्यतमं पतम्। प्रभविष्णोर्यथा तात क्षमा सर्वत्र सर्वदा॥
verses.translation: That man who pursues his object with perseverance and who is not annoyed at the long time a think takes to accomplish, that man is really great and gets unending happiness. It is my opinion that there is no better means of bringing in prosperity.

verses.sanskrit: क्षमेदशक्तः सर्वस्य शक्तिमान् धर्मकारणात्। अर्थानौ समौ यस्य तस्य नित्यं क्षमा हिता॥
verses.translation: Than for really strong man to exercise forgiveness at all times and at all places. The weak should forgive every body and the strong should forgive for the sake of virtue. He to whom the attainment and the non-attainment of an object is the same is ever forgiving.

verses.sanskrit: यत् सुखं सेवमानोऽपि धर्मार्थाभ्यां न हीयते। कामं तदुपसेवेत न मूढव्रतमाचरेत्॥
verses.translation: That happiness which when pursued dose not injure the virtue or the worldly profit of a man should be enjoyed one so may desire but he should not act like a fool (by giving himself up entirely to enjoyment).

verses.sanskrit: दुःखार्तेषु प्रमत्तेषु नास्तिकेष्वलसेषु च। न श्रीर्वसत्यदान्तेषु ये चोत्साहविवर्जिताः॥
verses.translation: In those that are overpowered by grief, in those that are too much attached to anything, in those that deny the existence of God and in those that are lazy, prosperity dose not reside, nor in those who have no control over their souls and who are devoid of all enthusiasm.

verses.sanskrit: आर्जवेन नरं युक्तमार्जवात् सव्यपत्रपम्। अशक्तं मन्यमानास्तु धर्षयन्ति कुबुद्धयः॥
verses.translation: Persons of bad intelligence oppress people endued with humility and modesty by reason of that humility, thinking them to be weak.

verses.sanskrit: अत्यार्यमतिदातारमतिशूरमतिव्रतम्। प्रज्ञाभिमानिनं चैव श्रीयानोपसर्पति॥
verses.translation: To those who are honest in the extreme, who give away in large measures, who are too heroic, who are of too firm determination and who are vain of their wisdom, prosperity never comes out of fear.

verses.sanskrit: न चातिगुणवत्स्वेषा नात्यन्तं निर्गुणेषु च। नैषा गुणान् कामयते नैर्गुण्यानानानुरज्यते। उन्मत्ता गौरिवान्धा श्रीः क्वचिदेवावतिष्ठते॥
verses.translation: Nor (dose prosperity reside) in persons endued with every virtue nor in those having none; she (prosperity) dose not desire a strong combination of good qualities. Prosperity resides in some one (without any fixed rule) like a mad cow.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निहोत्रफला वेदाः शीलवृत्तफलं श्रुतम्। रतिपुत्रफला नारी दत्तभुत्तफलं धनम्॥
verses.translation: The result of (the study of) the Vedas is the performance of the Home; the result of a knowledge of the holy books is good manners and good conduct. The fruits of a woman are the pleasures of co-habitation and offspring; and the consequences of wealth are the pleasure of gift and enjoyment.

verses.sanskrit: अधर्मोपार्जितैरथैर्यः करोत्यौदेहिकम्। न स तस्य फलं प्रेत्य भुङ्क्तेऽर्थस्य दुरागमात्॥
verses.translation: He who performs ceremonies for his good in the other world with wealth got by foul means. Dose not get the usual fruits of such ceremonies after death owing to the foul means by which the wealth was got.
